title: Graphic Designer (AU)
description:
Graphic Designer – Exhibitions, Events and Brand Activations



About the Role

We are looking for a talented and highly detail-oriented Graphic Designer to support the creative development and

delivery of exhibitions, events and brand activation projects.



This role is ideal for someone who can take a concept or creative direction and turn it into polished, on-brand design

outcomes, while also preparing accurate production-ready artwork based on dimensions, technical drawings, mark-ups

and artwork schedules.



The role requires a strong eye for design, excellent technical artwork skills, and a practical understanding of how

graphics are applied across physical environments and live experiences.

Experience in exhibitions, events, signage, environmental graphics or brand activations is highly preferred.



Key Responsibilities

• Create graphic design concepts and visual layouts based on client briefs, references and creative direction

• Roll out approved concepts across multipole assets and formats including graphics, signage, counters, walls,

plinths, digital screens, print collateral and branded materials

• Prepare accurate production-ready artwork files for print, signage and digital outputs

• Put together artwork schedules including dimensions, mark-ups working from technical details and production

specifications

• Apply brand guidelines consistency across a wide range of touchpoints

• Adapt master creative across multiple sizes and formats while maintaining design quality and consistency

• Ensure all files are accurate and ready for issue, including checking spelling, alignment, image quality, scale,

bleed, colour settings and layout consistency

• Maintain organised file structures, version control and clear naming conventions

• Communicate clearly and professionally with internal tea
mbers via Outlook, Teams and project updates

• Work collaborative with creative and production teams to meet deadlines and project needs

• Respond to feedback and revisions efficiently and accurately



Skills and Capabilities

• Strong graphic design fundamentals including typography, layout, composition, hierarchy and brand application

• Ability to translate concepts and creative direction into strong visual design outcomes

• Excellent art working skills and technical file setup

• High attention to detail and accuracy

• Ability to read and interpret artwork schedules, dimensions, technical drawings and production requirements

• Strong understanding of print production requirements including beleed, trim, safe zones, resolution and colour

modes

• Ability to design for both print and digital outputs

• Strong time management and ability to handle multiples tasks and deadlines

• Strong written English communications kills

• Ability to work independently while staying connected to a remote team

• Proactive approach to asking questions and flagging issues early



Software and tools

Essential

• Adobe illustrator

• Adobe InDesign

• Adobe Photoshop

• Adobe Acrobat

• Microsoft Outlook

• Microsoft Teams

• Microsoft Word

• Microsoft Excel

• Microsoft PowerPoint



Preferred / nice to have

• Adobe After Effects

• Adobe Premier Pro

• Figma

• Basic understanding of spatial or 3D design tools such as SketchUp

• Experience using shared devices, cloud-based file systems and structure folder management



Experience Required

• Minimum 3 years’ experience in graphic design, artwork production or a similar role

• Strong portfolio demonstrating both creative design and technical artwork capability

• Experience preparing files for print and production

• Experience working in a fast-paced studio, agency or project-based environment

• Experience in exhibitions, events, signage, retail environments, environmental graphics or brand activations is

highly preferred

• Experience working remotely with international teams is an advantage



Personal attributes

• Detail-orientated and organised

• Reliable and responsive

• Calm under pressure and able to manage deadlines

• Collaborative and easy to work with

• Positive attitude and willingness to learn

• Accountable for quality and accuracy

• Able to balance creativity with practical execution



What success looks like in a role

• Creative work is on-brand, well considered and visually strong

• Artwork files are accurate, organised and production-ready

• Deadlines are met and communication is clear

• Technical details are followed carefully reducing production issues and rework

• Design quality remains high across multiple formats and rollout

title: Hiring 4 Tax Accountants (AU)
description:
We believe our people are the foundation of our success. Our culture is built on respect, collaboration, and supporting one another.



We strive to create a workplace where employees feel valued, heard, and empowered to grow both personally and professionally. Our leadership is committed to fostering open communication, professional development, and a positive environment where everyone can perform at their best.



If you're looking for a company that values your contribution and supports your growth, we’d love to have you on our team.



Why Join Bravo?

We believe great people deserve great support. Here’s what you can expect when you join us:

-Competitive and stable full-time work from home opportunity

-HMO and Life Insurance coverage

-13th Month Pay

-Government Contributions (SSS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG)

-Sick Leave and Vacation Leave benefits

-Free Training and Professional Development opportunities

-Monthly Virtual Employee Engagement activities (with prizes!)

-Fun Company Get-Togethers

-An Annual Christmas Party that our team truly looks forward to

-Year-End Package



We make sure our team not only works hard, but also feels appreciated and celebrated.



About the Role

We are currently looking for four (4) experienced AU Tax Accountants to join our growing team.

In this role, you will work closely with an Australian firm handling tax compliance and accounting services for a diverse client base. This position offers the opportunity to further develop your expertise in Australian taxation while working in a supportive and collaborative environment.



Key Responsibilities

-Prepare and review Australian Tax Returns, BAS, and Financial Statements

-Ensure compliance with Australian tax regulations and legislation

-Provide tax planning support or advice to clients or internal stakeholders

-Analyze financial statements and provide insights where needed

-Maintain accurate financial records and documentation



What We’re Looking For

-2–5+ years of Australian Tax and Business Services experience

-Ability to prepare tax returns for Individuals, Partnerships, Companies (including Group Companies), and Trusts

-Experience in preparing and reviewing Financial Statements

-Solid understanding of Australian tax legislation, including:

Capital Gains Tax (CGT)

Fringe Benefits Tax (FBT)

Division 7A compliance and application

-Experience in preparing IAS and BAS

-Experience in tax planning is an advantage

-Strong analytical and communication skills

-Proficient in using Xero, XPM, and other accounting software

-Positive and proactive attitude with a fun team spirit

-Bookkeeping experience is a plus



We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Remote Productivity Assistant/Coach (Part-Time)
description:
# Remote Productivity Assistant (Part-Time)



**$2/hour | 3 hours/day | Tuesday–Friday (USA)**



---



I'm an independent consultant based in the US. I run multiple projects from home and I'm looking for a remote productivity assistant to help me stay focused and track my morning work block.



This role is part accountability, part monitoring. You'll monitor my work sessions via video call and workspace cameras, log what you observe, check in with me regularly, and be available when I need to talk something through. If I'm not on the call at the start of the shift, you reach out and get things going.



The only deliverable is a simple log you fill out every 5–10 minutes. The rest is showing up, paying attention, and keeping the energy going.



**What you'll do:**

- Monitor my work session via Google Meet, WhatsApp, or workspace cameras depending on the situation

- Check in with me regularly throughout the shift

- Log what you observe using a simple form I'll provide

- Be available to talk — sometimes I need to think out loud or just have someone there

- If I'm not on the call when the shift starts, you reach out until I am



**Good fit if you:**

- Take initiative — you don't wait around, you check in

- Bring energy and encouragement. Think coach energy.

- Are outgoing and comfortable talking to people

- Can be direct when something needs to be said

- Want to practice conversational English — I'm very comfortable with ESL speakers

- Are reliable and show up on time every day

- Can handle a mix of conversation and quiet



**Tech requirements:**

- Stable internet 

- A laptop or desktop with at least 4GB RAM that can run a video call and a camera app at the same time

- A webcam and mic - built-in is fine

- **A space where you can talk freely from 1am–4am PHT**



**Not a VA role.** No admin, no research, no scheduling.



**Schedule:** Tuesday–Friday, 9am–12pm PDT (12am–3am PHT)

**Pay:** $2/hour

**Duration:** Ongoing



---



**To apply:** Fill out the application form here: 



---



**Tags:** body doubling, ADHD, productivity, accountability partner, cheerleader, motivational support, morning accountability, customer service, English conversation practice, ESL, remote coworking, life coaching, focus partner, time tracking, work from home, graveyard shift, night shift, positive attitude, energy, enthusiasm, video monitoring, CCTV monitoring, remote monitoring

We are an international media agency managing multiple creators across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube. We’re looking for a long-term video editor who can transform raw content into high-performing short-form videos and YouTube edits.

title: Full Time Remote Chat Operator
description:
This position is fully remote and ideal for candidates who are comfortable working in a fast-paced messaging environment.

title: Online English/World History Teachers (Near-Native Accent)
description:
bout Pomelo Academy

Pomelo Academy is an innovative online international school dedicated to delivering high-quality education to students worldwide. We provide a dynamic and engaging learning environment, offering individualized instruction that transcends geographical boundaries.



Job Description

We are seeking passionate and experienced near-native English-speaking online teachers with strong backgrounds in English and/or World History. Candidates may specialize in one subject (English or History) or be able to teach both. The ideal teachers will have experience working with young learners and be committed to creating a supportive and engaging online classroom.



Responsibilities

• Provide instruction in English and/or World History to individuals and small groups of 2–6 students aged 5–18.

• Utilize international-standard curricula.

• Create and deliver engaging lesson plans tailored to each student’s needs.

• Monitor and assess student progress, providing regular feedback to students and parents.

• Foster a positive and encouraging learning environment.



Qualifications

• Professional teacher with at least 2 years of teaching experience (Kindergarten–Grade 12) and verifiable teaching certifications such as TESOL, TEFL, etc.

• Near-native English accent.

• Strong academic background in English and/or World History (additional experience in Science/Economics is a plus).

• Ability to work between 17:00–22:00 (Weekdays) and 09:00–22:00 (Weekends) GMT+8.

• Experience teaching in public/international schools.

• Skilled at working with young learners aged 5–18

• Familiarity with ClassIn and digital literacy (Microsoft Suite and other educational platforms).

• Must have a stable and fast internet connection and a PC/laptop with at least Windows 10/Mac OS Catalina.



Benefits

• Flexible working hours.

• Opportunity to teach using internationally recognized curricula.

• Work with an international team of educators.

• Professional development and training opportunities.

• Regular enrollment system (not booking-based), ensuring a stable and consistent teaching schedule.

title: Phone Videographer
description:
Earn ?12,000/month filming products with your phone at your local mall.



No experience needed. No editing needed. No talking needed. Just your smartphone and a steady hand.



We run a product review channel. We need someone in Manila or Cebu to film short, silent product clips at SM Mall, Robinsons, or any large electronics/home goods store. We handle all editing, voiceover, and post-production — you just film.



PAY:

- Fixed ?12,000/month

- Minimum 300 product videos per month (about 8 mall visits)

- Paid monthly via PayPal

- You choose your own schedule — go whenever it's convenient for you



WHAT YOU'LL DO:



1. Go to any large mall near you

2. Find products on the shelves and film them. All categories are welcome as long as the product sells on Amazon US. That said, some categories earn more for us than others, so when you have a choice, here's the priority:



   TOP TIER: Headphones, earbuds, portable speakers, soundbars, projectors, kitchen appliances (air fryers, blenders, coffee machines)

   STRONG: Home improvement tools, smart home devices, beauty products, office equipment

   GOOD: Toys, pet products, outdoor gear, furniture, home decor, garden tools



   Everything else is still worth filming — if it's on a shelf and it's on Amazon US, film it.



3. Film the price tag or product label clearly and up close (5 sec)

4. Slowly walk around the product — show the build quality, materials, buttons, ports, packaging, and any features or accessories (60-90 sec)

5. Upload the clips to a shared Google Drive folder



IMPORTANT FILMING RULES:



- No talking in the video. Silent footage only.

- Wait until the aisle is clear before filming. No other shoppers should be visible in the shot.

- Keep the camera steady and move slowly. Think "product showcase", not vlog.

- Never film the same product twice. If you've already filmed it, skip it and find something new.

- Before filming, quickly check if the product is sold on Amazon US (I will show you how — it takes 5 seconds with an app on your phone).



WHAT GETS REJECTED (does not count toward your 300):

- Blurry or shaky video

- Product label not clearly readable

- Other people visible in the shot

- Duplicate product (already filmed before)

- Products not sold on Amazon US



I will send you example videos I filmed myself so you can see exactly what a good clip looks like. The bar is not high — just clear, steady, and focused on the product.



This is perfect for students, freelancers, or anyone who visits malls regularly and wants easy extra income on their own schedule.



TO APPLY: Film a quick test video of any product near you right now. Show the label up close, then slowly walk around the product for 30 seconds. No talking. Attach it to your application.

title: Part-time product video filmer - work from your local mall
description: I'm looking for someone in Manila or Cebu to film short product videos inside SM Mall, Robinsons, or any large electronics/home goods store near you. No experience needed. You only need a smartphone with a decent camera. WHAT YOU'LL DO: - Visit a large store 1-2x per week - Film 60-90 second clips of products on the shelf - First shot must be the price tag or product label, clearly readable - Then slowly walk around the product, close-ups of features and accessories - Name each video folder after the product (e.g. "Sony Headphones WH1000XM5") - Upload to Google Drive and share with me PAY: - $0.20 per approved product video, paid weekly via PayPal - Bonus: $2 for every 20 approved products in one session - Blurry, wrong product, or missing label shot = not paid REALISTIC EARNINGS: A 2-3 hour session filming 40 products = $8-10 plus bonuses. You choose when you go. TO APPLY: Send a short test video of any product near you. Film the label clearly, then walk around the product for 30 seconds.

title: E-Commerce Email Designer
description:
This is NOT a drag-and-drop design job.



We need a designer who THINKS about what they're creating. Someone who reads copy and instinctively knows how to restructure it visually so people actually read it. Someone who spots awkward phrasing or AI-sounding bullshit and fixes it. 



Someone who turns walls of text into scannable, engaging layouts that drive clicks.



If you just paste copy into Figma and make it "look nice" - this role isn't for you.

If you actively improve every
 you touch - keep reading.



WHO WE ARE:

We're an
 marketing agency helping premium DTC brands add additional revenue through
. Our
s don't just look good - they look SO GOOD, THEY SELL & MAKE ECOM CUSTOMERS FALL IN LOVE.



THE PROBLEM YOU'LL SOLVE:

Our copywriters write the messaging. But copy alone doesn't convert. YOU transform that copy into visual experiences that:

- Guide the reader's eye exactly where we want it

- Break up text so it's actually readable on mobile

- Create visual rhythm that keeps people scrolling

- Make the CTA impossible to miss



WHAT MAKES THIS ROLE DIFFERENT:

You're not just a designer. You're a visual editor. When you receive copy, you:

- Read it critically (Does this flow? Does this make sense?)

- Restructure it visually (How do I break this into digestible chunks?)

- Flag issues proactively ("This section is confusing - here's how I'd reword it")

- Add visual storytelling (Icons, dividers, imagery that reinforces the message)

- Optimize for scanning (Most people skim - make key points pop)

- FIND what you need to make it great (Missing a product image? You go find it. Need a lifestyle shot? You source it. You don't wait to be spoon-fed every asset - you take initiative to make the
 as good as it can be.)



WHAT YOU'LL DESIGN:

- 25
s per week (5+ per day)

- Campaign
s: Product launches, sales, promotions, content

- Flow
s: Welcome series, abandoned cart, post-purchase, winback

- Style: Premium, high-converting, mobile-first



WHAT "HIGH QUALITY" MEANS TO US:

- Visual hierarchy that guides the eye (not everything screaming for attention)

- White space used strategically (breathing room, not emptiness)

- Text broken into scannable chunks (no walls of copy)

- Pattern interrupts that keep readers engaged (not the same layout repeated)

- CTAs that stand out without being obnoxious

- Mobile-first design (thumb-friendly, fast-loading)

- Brand consistency across every



Examples of the quality we expect: 



WHAT WE DON'T WANT:

- Generic templates with copy pasted in

- Designs that look pretty but ignore readability & conversion

- "Set it and forget it" mentality

- Designers who wait to be told what to fix

- Big blocks of text because "that's what the copy said"



REQUIREMENTS:

- 1+ years designing e-commerce
s (show me
s, not websites)

- Portfolio showing BEFORE/AFTER or explaining your design decisions

- Figma proficiency

- Strong eye for typography and spacing

- Understanding of
 rendering constraints

- Fluent English (you'll be suggesting copy edits)

- Can commit to 9
s weekly, consistently



YOU'LL STAND OUT IF YOU:

- Can articulate WHY you made specific design choices

- Have reworded or restructured copy to improve an

- Understand what makes people click (not just what looks good)

- Notice things others miss (alignment, spacing, flow issues)

- Are opinionated about design (and can back it up)

- Have worked with premium/luxury brands



COMPENSATION:

$400USD /month



WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU:

- Creative ownership (we want your input, not just execution)

- Work with premium brands (portfolio pieces you'll be proud of)

- Consistent weekly volume (predictable income)

- Direct feedback loop (we share what's converting)

- Growth path to Senior Designer / Art Director as we scale



HOW TO APPLY:

1. Send your portfolio 
 designs specifically)

2. Tell me:



   - What's the difference between a good
 design and a great one?

   - Tell me about a time you improved copy or layout beyond what was asked

   - What do most
 designers get wrong?



(You can use AI to answer these questions; however, I want your real, sincere answers, not AI bullshit please)



3. Include 5-8 examples of your best
 work (the more the better)



Serious candidates will complete a test project before hiring:

- We provide: Brand guidelines + product images + brief

- You design: 1 complete

- Timeline: 24 hours



We respond to all applications within 48 hours.





REAL TALK:



I want someone who actually cares about getting better at this. Not just clocking in.



If you're that person, I'll invest in you. Train you properly. Help you build a portfolio that opens doors. The goal is we both grow from this.

title: Shopify Plus Developer + CRO Funnel Specialist

IMPORTANT

Start your application with the word: GROWTH



We are hiring candidates who understand both Shopify development AND revenue performance.

title: Content Manager (Social Media Management, Video Editing, Graphic Design, Email Marketing etc)

title: YouTube Thumbnail Designer (CTR-Focused) – $5 Per Thumbnail
description:
I am looking for a sharp, data-driven Thumbnail Designer who understands that a thumbnail’s only job is to get the click. I don’t just want "nice" designs; I want high-performance visuals that stop the scroll.



This is a gig-based role. Please note: For most videos, I will require at least 2 different thumbnail variations (different concepts/angles), which will be paid at $5 USD each ($10 total for the pair).



Pay: $5 USD per individual thumbnail.

Tools: Photoshop, Canva, or AI-assisted workflows are welcome, provided the final result is high-quality and follows YouTube best practices.



What I am looking for:



CTR Awareness: You understand visual hierarchy and how to use high-contrast colors to stand out on a crowded feed.



Psychology of the Click: You know how to create curiosity or emotional triggers through imagery and minimal text (3 words or less).



Mobile-First Design: Your text must be readable and your focal point must be clear even on a small phone screen.



Efficiency: I am looking for someone who has a fast, reliable workflow and can deliver variations quickly.



Responsibilities:



Create custom thumbnails (usually 2 variations per video) based on provided titles/briefs.



Incorporate provided assets (photos/branding) efficiently.



Standard turnaround: 24–48 hours.



How to Apply:



Send a link to your Portfolio (specifically YouTube thumbnails).



Tell me: What is one technique you use to increase CTR?



Include the word "CLICK" in your subject line so I know you read the full post.

title: Senior Temu Growth Partner – US LLC – High Profit Share
description:
About the Role & Infrastructure

Verified US LLC with Mercury Bank.



Shopify Collective supply chain with authentic US-based suppliers.

I am NOT looking for a general VA. I am looking for a Growth Shark who has personally scaled Temu stores (Semi-Managed) to 5 and 6-figure monthly profits.



The Mission

Your sole responsibility is the P&L. You are the driver of this "Ferrari".



Break the Stagnation: Move the store from 4 impressions to high-velocity sales within the first 48-72 hours.



Algorithm Manipulation: Execute the 'Price-Gap Sequence' and 'Velocity Seeding' to trigger the Temu 'New Arrival' boost.



Automation Mastery: Maintain zero-latency inventory and tracking sync between Shopify Collective and Temu using advanced Middleware/API.



Requirements (No Exceptions)

Compensation

HOW TO APPLY (The Evidence Test)

Start your application with the words "LEKER SHARK."

Adv. Matan Leker

Owner, Leker Projects LLC

title: Kajabi Web Designer Expert
description:
Web Developer & Designer Needed to Rebuild The Shala School of Yoga Website on Kajabi 

Project Type: One-off project with potential ongoing hourly work 

Experience Level: Intermediate to Expert 



 

About Us 

The Shala School of Yoga (TSSY) is a well-established UK-based yoga and wellness organisation offering: 

Yoga teacher trainings (200- and 300-hour programs) 

Continuing professional development (CPD) courses 

Yoga Retreats 



We are ready to refresh our online presence by rebuilding our website on Kajabi, moving away from WordPress, to create a clean, modern, and easy-to-manage site that fully reflects our brand and offerings. 



Scope of Work 

We are looking for an experienced Kajabi Web Developer & Designer with strong design sensibility to: 

- Rebuild our existing WordPress site into Kajabi 

- Recommend the best Kajabi templates or customisations for our needs 

- Design a polished, modern, user-friendly site that aligns with TSSY’s brand 

- Build a structure that is easy for our team to update and maintain 

- Integrate required third-party tools, including 
nce plug ins

- Set up clear navigation and intuitive site architecture 

- Preserve and migrate all existing SEO work (page titles, meta descriptions, headings, alt text) 

- Implement 301 redirects for any changed URLs to maintain search engine rankings 

Note: This is not a full-from-scratch design job. We would like to work from Kajabi templates so long as it feels professional and cohesive. 



Required Experience 

- Experience working with Kajabi or similar template-based platforms 

- 3+ years professional web development experience 

- Strong design skills; able to produce clean, modern, stylistically consistent websites 

- Experience with website rebuilds, domain migrations, and third-party integrations 

- Excellent communication skills in English 

- High attention to detail, reliability, and responsiveness 



How to Apply 

To be considered, please include all of the following in your proposal: 

- One portfolio link 

- Links to 1–3 websites you personally built in Kajabi that are most relevant to this project 

A short paragraph (3–5 sentences) commenting on our current site. Note that we will only be transitioning the About Us, Teacher Training Section and Blogs to the new site. 

What works well? 

What is one thing you would improve in the rebuild? 



Applications missing any of the above will not be considered. 

You do not need to submit a full proposal yet — this will be requested only from one or two shortlisted candidates. 



Next Steps 

Shortlisted candidates will be invited to prepare: 

- A fixed-fee proposal for the complete project build 

- An hourly rate for ongoing support and maintenance 





What We Offer 

- Opportunity to shape the online presence of a well-established wellness brand 

- Friendly and collaborative communication (we have two tea
mbers in the Philippines!) 

- Clear brief and efficient project management 

- Potential long-term working relationship after launch 



Additional Considerations 

- Redesign logo or keep current one: Current Logo Link 

- Ensure site is mobile-responsive and optimised for SEO

title: WEB DEVELOPER - WordPress/Elementor
description:
WordPress / Elementor Developer — Freelance 



We’re looking for an experienced WordPress/Elementor developer to build two websites from provided design files. This is a freelance, remote role billed hourly. Candidates based anywhere are welcome, but you must be available for at least a few hours overlap with UK business hours (GMT/BST) for communication and collaboration. 

 

About the project 

The Shala yoga studio and The Shala School of Yoga were once under the same directorship and shared a single website (
). They are now two separate businesses, each with their own director. The shared site needs to be separated into two distinct sites: 

— 
 — for Vahni at The Shala  for  yoga studio classes and workshops 

— The Shala School of Yoga under theshalalondon address, for teacher trainings  

 

The project will be divided into two phases as follows and you will bill the two businesses respectively  

 

PHASE 1 – VAHNI  

What you’ll be doing 

— Archiving the existing site properly so all data is retained and retrievable  

 

— Building a WordPress/Elementor Pro site from provided design files  (Figma/HTML mockups)

 

— Working closely with our team on a clear design handoff and building faithfully to that spec 

 

— Migrating and organising content from the existing shared WordPress site into the new site 

 

— Integrating Momence as the booking platform in the same way it is integrated currently 

 

— Setting up a dynamic events/workshop system with SEO-optimised content in WordPress (not reliant on Momence for indexing) 

 

— Implementing SEO migration — preserving existing URLs, 301 redirects, and applying meta data provided by our SEO team 

 

— Ensuring strong UX throughout — the sites must be clean, fast, accessible, and easy to navigate 

 

PHASE 2 – THE SHALA SCHOOL BUILD 

What you’ll be doing 

— Building a WordPress/Elementor Pro site from provided HTML templates 

 

— Working closely with the team on any updates/design tweaks 

 

— Migrating and organising content from the existing WordPress site into the new site 

 

— Integrating Momence as the booking platform – the integration here is very straightforward. 

 

— Implementing SEO migration — preserving existing URLs, 301 redirects, and applying meta data provided by our SEO team 

 

— Ensuring strong UX throughout — the site must be clean, fast, accessible, and easy to navigate  

 

 

What we’re looking for: 

— Strong WordPress and Elementor Pro experience — you must be proficient, not just familiar. Minimum 5 years experience 

 

— Solid knowledge of WordPress plugins — you should know which tools to use and why 

 

— Comfortable building from a designer’s Figma handoff and/or HTML mockups — your role is to build to spec, not interpret design direction 

 

— Experience with booking platform integrations - we work with Momence

 

— Understanding of SEO best practices in WordPress builds 

 

— Strong UX sensibility — you build sites that feel considered, not just functional 

 

— Clear communicator & excellent English — we’ll be working remotely and need daily progress updates 

 

— Available for at least a few hours overlap with UK business hours 

 

Nice to have 

— Familiarity with The Events Calendar or similar WordPress events plugins 

 

— Experience with WPX hosting 

 

To apply 

Please share  

- a link to your portfolio 

- two or three examples of the best WordPress/Elementor sites you’ve built 

- your hourly rate 

- a brief note on your experience with booking system integrations and working from design handoffs would be helpful. 

 

We’re moving quickly on this — responsive candidates will be prioritised. 



*There may be opportunities for ongoing support and maintenance work following launch.

title: Cold Email & GTM Engineer
description:
About GenFlows

GenFlows is a GTM engineering and cold outbound agency based in the Netherlands. We build transparent, client-owned outbound systems using Clay, cold
 infrastructure, and LinkedIn automation. Our partners span B2B verticals including MCA/lending, SaaS, fintech, M&A advisory, and professional services. We operate on a Glass Box philosophy: every system we build, the client owns and understands.

The Role

You will own end-to-end outbound campaign delivery for a dedicated portfolio of client accounts. This means building Clay enrichment and qualification tables, writing cold
 copy, managing sending infrastructure, and continuously optimizing campaign performance. You work directly with clients and collaborate closely with our Delivery Lead to maintain GenFlows quality standards.

What You Will Do

Build and maintain Clay tables for lead enrichment, ICP classification, and qualification scoring across client accounts

Set up and manage cold
 infrastructure (domains, mailboxes, warmup, DNS, deliverability monitoring)

Write, A/B test, and iterate on cold
 sequences tailored to each client's ICP and value proposition

Execute LinkedIn outbound campaigns where applicable, coordinating messaging across channels

Integrate Clay workflows with client CRMs (HubSpot, Salesforce) via webhooks and native integrations

Monitor campaign metrics (open rates, reply rates, bounce rates, SQLs) and present weekly performance reports to clients

Collaborate with the Delivery Lead on campaign strategy, ICP refinement, and copywriting frameworks

Document all systems and processes in line with the Glass Box methodology so clients retain full ownership

What You Bring

Required:

1+ year hands-on experience with 
 (building tables, using enrichment providers, writing Clay prompts, integrating webhooks)

Proven track record running cold
 campaigns with measurable results (reply rates, meetings booked, SQLs generated)

Working knowledge of
 infrastructure: domain setup, SPF/DKIM/DMARC, warmup tools, inbox rotation, and deliverability best practices

Strong copywriting skills for B2B cold outreach across different verticals and buyer personas

Experience with at least one CRM (HubSpot preferred) and basic understanding of data flows between systems

Self-directed work style with clear communication; comfortable working async in a remote team

Nice to Have:

Experience with LinkedIn outbound tools (Expandi, Dripify, or similar)

Familiarity with Instantly, Smartlead, or other cold
 sending platforms

Understanding of B2B verticals like MCA/lending, SaaS, or financial services

Basic API knowledge (REST, webhooks, JSON) for building integrations

Experience working in an agency setting managing multiple client accounts simultaneously

How We Work

Small, focused team: you are not a number. Your work has direct, visible impact on client results and agency growth.

Weekly cadences with the Delivery Lead for campaign reviews, strategy alignment, and knowledge sharing.

Full autonomy within your client accounts. We trust you to make decisions and flag issues proactively.

Glass Box methodology: we build systems that are transparent and documented. No black boxes, no lock-in.

Tools of the trade: Clay, Instantly/Smartlead, HubSpot, LinkedIn, Slack, Notion.

How to Apply

Send a short video intro and your relevant experience with your application. Apply here: 
. 



Optional: Include a brief description of a Clay table or cold
 campaign you built and the results it achieved. No formal CV required -- we care about what you have built and how you think.

title: AI-First Customer Service Specialist (E-commerce)
description:
Contract type: Freelance / Part-time — potential to grow to full-time

Hours per week: 15–25 hrs to start

Location: Remote

Start date: ASAP





About this role

We're a fast-growing direct-to-consumer e-commerce brand looking for one sharp person to take full ownership of our customer service — built on AI.



We're moving away from an external CS agency and rebuilding our support operation around Minimal AI, an AI customer support platform that automates 90%+ of tickets. Your job is to make that system work as well as possible — and handle the small percentage of cases it can't.



This is not a traditional CS role. You won't be answering tickets all day. You'll be training the AI, improving its responses, and only stepping in when real human judgment is needed.





What you'll do

Training & improving the AI (80% of your time)

• Review AI-generated ticket responses and flag what's wrong, incomplete, or off-brand

• Give structured feedback to the Minimal AI Manager so it learns and improves

• Write and update the protocols the AI follows — return policies, edge cases, tone of voice

• Identify patterns: when the AI keeps getting something wrong, you fix the root cause

• Expand AI coverage over time — the goal is 90%+ resolution rate, you own that number





Handling what the AI can't (20% of your time)

• Complex complaints that need real judgment, not a script

• Sensitive situations where the customer relationship is at stake

• Escalations, high-value orders, or cases that need a personal response





Reporting

• Post a short daily summary in Slack: volume, AI resolution rate, anything notable

• Track KPIs weekly and update our tracker — no chasing required, you just do it

• Flag recurring issues or product complaints to the founders





Requirements

• Hands-on experience with AI tools in a professional setting — not just personal use

• Background in e-commerce customer service for brands doing over 5M annual revenue — you know what good looks like

• You understand that giving AI structured, specific feedback is a skill — and you have it

• Strong written English — you'll be communicating with customers and writing AI protocols.

• Self-directed — you don't need to be managed day-to-day.





Nice to have

• Experience with Gorgias, Zendesk, or similar helpdesk tools

• Shopify knowledge — order flows, refunds, fulfilment logic

• You've trained or fine-tuned an AI tool in a structured way before

• DTC brand experience





KPIs you'll own

• AI Resolution Rate — target 85%+ within 60 days, 90%+ within 90 days

• First Response Time — under 2 hours for human-handled tickets

• CSAT Score — above 4.2 / 5.0

• Refund Rate — under 5%, flag if trending up



One thing to know upfront

This role will look different in 12 months. As the AI gets smarter, the manual volume drops. Your job evolves from doing to managing and improving the system.

If that sounds like a problem, this isn't the right fit. If that sounds interesting, apply.

title: Short Form Video Clipper for Horror YouTube Channel
description:
Hi I'm Kat! I run a YouTube channel called Kat Watches Horror Movies where I watch scary films and scream my way through them. I have 116K+ subscribers, over 1.2 million views in the last 90 days, and a growing audience who genuinely love the chaotic energy of watching someone react to their favourite movies and get scared. I'm building out my online presence and I need help! I'm looking to hire 2–3 dedicated clippers to take my long-form YouTube videos and turn them into short, punchy, scroll-stopping clips for YouTube shorts, Instagram Reels, TikTok, and X.



Your Role:

- Watch my YouTube videos (full reactions are usually 30–50 min) and find the BEST moments-- big scares, funny reactions, dramatic commentary, chaotic energy, then edit them into 30–90 second vertical reels (9:16 format) with captions and clean cuts.

- Deliver 5–10 clips per week, ready to post 

- Match the vibe of my channel, lots of clips already up on YouTube so you can get a sense. 



Looking for someone who:

- Is experienced with short-form video editing. 

- Is great at adding captions (accurate, readable)

- Understands what makes a clip go viral (pacing, hooks, the right moment to cut)

- Reliable and communicative!

- Can turn out 5-10 videos per week



NO AI CLIPPING! THAT'S WHY I'M HERE! I already have Opus AI and have tried a bunch of these platforms already. At this point I can spot an AI clip a mile away!



MAJOR BONUS POINTS if you like/understand horror content, and you're funny!!



Please include the following in your application:

- Tell me who you are, your editing experience, and whether you've worked with creators before.

- 2–3 examples of short-form clips you've made (Send links! If you have a portfolio, or an account you've worked on, send it! Do you work on your own Instagram or YouTube? Send it!)



I'll be hiring 2–3 people from this posting! Excited to see your portfolios! Hoping to start ASAP!

title: Social Media Manager — NYC Korean Restaurants | Full-Time Remote
description:
We're 2020 Hindsight Marketing, a NYC-based agency managing social media for 6 Korean restaurants — from high-end speakeasies and upscale dessert boutiques to modern Korean dining. We need a full-time, Philippines-based Social Media Manager who can handle influencer outreach, content creation, and community management across Instagram and TikTok.



What you'll actually do every day:

You'll manage influencer outreach for NYC restaurants — identifying the right influencers, evaluating their audience (not just their follower count), writing personalized DMs, and tracking every interaction across multiple Google Sheets. You'll handle content calendars, community engagement, and review responses across 6 restaurant accounts. You'll create Instagram posters and graphics in Canva and edit short-form video for Reels and TikTok. You'll source trending restaurant content on TikTok and log reference videos for our content pipeline.





The hardest parts of this job:

-Influencer judgment. Our restaurants range from a $1M-renovation dessert boutique on 5th Avenue to an Eater Top 10 speakeasy to a casual Korean pub. The influencer who's perfect for one is completely wrong for another. You need to look at an influencer's content, their audience, their vibe, and know which restaurant they belong with. This requires cultural awareness of the NYC dining scene — not just knowing what Korean food is, but understanding the difference between a premium modern Korean restaurant and a casual KBBQ spot, and why that distinction matters for marketing.



-Design and content quality. NYC's restaurant marketing scene is extremely competitive and constantly evolving. The posters, graphics, and videos you create need to look like they belong on a premium NYC restaurant's feed — not like a generic Canva template. You need a strong visual eye, an understanding of what's trending, and the ability to match your design style to each restaurant's unique brand and positioning.





What we need from you:

-Video editing skills (CapCut, Premiere, or similar — you'll be creating Reels and TikToks)

-Graphic design skills (Canva minimum, Photoshop/Illustrator a plus — you'll be creating posters and social media graphics that need to look premium)

-Strong English writing that sounds natural, not translated (Gen-Z/Millennial American tone)

-Google Sheets proficiency (data entry, filtering, basic formulas — you'll live in spreadsheets)

-Experience managing Instagram and/or TikTok accounts for food, restaurants, or hospitality

-Comfort working through an AI chatbot for daily task management (this is non-negotiable)

-Attention to detail — multiple sheets must be updated in parallel for every influencer action. Miss one update and the entire pipeline breaks.



Schedule & logistics:

Full-time, remote

Working hours: 12:00 PM – 8:00 PM EST (1:00 AM – 9:00 AM Philippines Time)

Daily time tracking via Timeproof is required

You'll work on a dedicated agency MacBook via remote access



Who thrives here:

People who like structure. People who follow instructions precisely and ask questions when something is unclear instead of guessing. People who take pride in doing things right, not just doing things fast. People who can look at a restaurant's Instagram and immediately see what's working and what isn't.



Who does NOT thrive here:

People who treat checklists as checkboxes without doing the actual work. People who mark things "done" when they aren't. People who want creative freedom without oversight. If you need constant hand-holding to start tasks, or if you disappear without communicating, this won't work.



To apply:

Submit your application and we'll send you a short screening form (takes about 10 minutes). Candidates who advance will be invited to a paid video interview and test. We compensate your time for the final evaluation,  we respect the effort!

4) Upload Products to POD Platform

5) Sync Products to Etsy & Shopify

This includes:



syncing to Etsy

syncing to Shopify

organizing listings

making sure listings are complete and accurate

checking that images, titles, variants, and pricing are correct

6) Create Product Mockups & Videos



You will create strong product visuals that help products convert.



This includes:



product mockup images

lifestyle mockups

clean e-commerce product images

short product videos / mockup videos when possible



We want visuals that are:



clean

modern

eye-catching

optimized for Etsy / Shopify / ads / social media

7) Write SEO Titles, Descriptions & Tags



You will write SEO-optimized listing content for each product.



This includes:



product titles

product descriptions

Etsy tags / keywords

You should understand how to write listings that help products rank and convert.



Examples of what we want:

strong keyword targeting for Etsy and Google search

Ideal Candidate



We are looking for someone who is:



creative and internet-savvy

familiar with crypto culture, 
s, and online communities

able to move fast without sacrificing quality

comfortable using AI tools to improve output

able to identify products that have real sales potential

organized and detail-oriented

experienced with e-commerce and print-on-demand workflows

Required Skills



You should have experience with most of the following:



graphic design for apparel or print-on-demand

crypto / finance / 
 culture understanding

Etsy product listing optimization

Shopify product listing workflow

product mockups and presentation

AI-assisted content or design tools

transparent PNG and print-ready file creation

basic SEO / keyword research

trend spotting and product research

Preferred Experience



Strong preference for candidates with experience in one or more of these:

Shopify print-on-demand stores

crypto or finance merchandise

viral 
 products

Canva / Photoshop / Illustrator / Kittl

ChatGPT / AI prompt writing

POD platforms such as Printify, Printful, CustomCat, etc.



Deliverables / Weekly Expectations



Expected weekly output may include:

trend research / competitor research

mockups and listing visuals

SEO titles, tags, and descriptions



(Exact workload may vary depending on experience and efficiency.)

title: AI VIDEO EXPERT [Need to be great at prompting]
description:
AI Video Creator — Realistic UGC-Style Video for App Ads

We run a portfolio of consumer apps and need someone who can generate hyper-realistic, casual UGC-style video using Sora, VEO, or Kling. The output needs to look like a real person filmed it on their phone — not like AI. If it looks AI-generated, it's useless to us.

Think: someone casually talking about an app, using a product in their kitchen, walking through a store, reacting to something on their phone. That level of realism is what we need.



The Work



Generate realistic AI video for app ads and organic TikTok based on briefs and scripts we provide

Stay obsessively up to date on the latest prompting techniques, model updates, and workflows for Sora, VEO, and Kling — these tools evolve weekly and we need someone who's on top of every update

Know which tool to use for which type of output and why

Iterate fast until the output passes as real casual footage





Must-Haves



Proven experience generating realistic human video with Sora, VEO, or Kling (not Runway, not Pika — we're specifically looking for these three)

You actively follow model updates, new techniques, and community discoveries for these tools — if we ask you what changed in the last update, you should know

Output that consistently passes as real phone-shot UGC, not polished AI demos

Weekend availability (Saturday + Sunday required)

Quick turnarounds


 communication via
 — must understand English

Reliable internet



Nice-to-Haves



Basic editing skills to trim and polish AI output

Experience creating content specifically for mobile app ads

Active in AI video communities 
/X, 
, Reddit) where new methods get shared first





Pay

Competitive monthly rate paid via Paypal. Long-term ongoing role.



Work hours:

You can work during phillipine time.



How to Apply

Applications that skip these steps get ignored.

Subject line must include the word RED.

Answer these questions in your message:



Which of the three tools (Sora, VEO, Kling) are you strongest with and why?

What's one specific prompting technique or workflow you use to make AI video look less like AI?

How do you stay current on updates and new methods for these tools?

Confirm you can work Saturdays and Sundays.



Send 3+ examples of realistic, casual-style AI video you generated. Direct links or files only. If we can immediately tell it's AI, you won't move forward.



What Happens Next

Qualified applicants get a paid test — a brief and a deadline. You get paid regardless of outcome.

Hiring asap.

title: Senior Video Editor (YouTube & Short-Form) | Long-Term | Client Work  Job Description
description:
We are hiring high-level video editors for long-term work under Khun Camp, a YouTube growth and content systems company.



This is not beginner work. You will be working on real client projects, including YouTube channels, short-form content, and monetized media systems.



We are looking for editors who:

• Understand retention, pacing, and storytelling

• Can edit both long-form (8–20 min) and short-form (TikTok, Reels, Shorts)

• Use subtitles strategically (word-by-word, not blocks)

• Know how to enhance emotion, not just cut clips

• Can work independently without constant instruction



You will be part of a system where your edits directly impact views, retention, and revenue.



Responsibilities



• Edit YouTube videos with strong pacing and structure

• Create high-retention short-form clips from long-form content

• Add clean subtitles, sound effects, and background music (no copyrighted or lyrical tracks unless approved)

• Follow branding and content style guidelines

• Deliver on time without excuses



Requirements



• Proven portfolio (YouTube or client work required)

• Strong understanding of retention editing

• Fast turnaround time

• Good communication and professionalism

• Ability to follow systems and improve over time



Trial Task (Required)



All applicants must complete a 48-hour test:



We will give you raw footage.

You will:

• Edit 1 short-form video

• Edit 1 long-form segment (3–5 minutes)



We are testing:

• Retention

• Pacing

• Creativity

• Clean execution



Compensation



Monthly or per-project (based on skill level)



Top performers will:

• Get consistent client work

• Be placed into revenue-generating pipelines

• Scale into higher-paying roles



Important



We are not looking for freelancers who disappear.

We are building a long-term team to support paying clients.



If you are serious about growth, consistency, and real work, apply.



Business Name



Khun Camp



Applications without portfolio or that feel AI-generated will be ignored.

title: Virtual Assistant / Operations Coordinator (Support Two Executives)| Part-Time (5–10 hrs/wk to start, with growth
description:
About the Role



We are seeking a highly capable, detail-oriented Virtual Assistant / Operations Coordinator to support two executives across a portfolio of business ventures and personal and family responsibilities.



This is not a typical admin role.



You will act as a central operator—bringing structure, clarity, and execution across multiple moving parts. You will work closely with both principals to ensure priorities are managed, communication flows seamlessly, and nothing falls through the cracks.



If you are proactive, highly organised, and take ownership of outcomes—not just tasks—this role will suit you.



What Success Looks Like

• Calendars, travel, and family commitments run seamlessly

• Financial admin and reporting are accurate and up to date

• Communication across projects is clear, timely, and proactive

• We spend less time on coordination, and more time on high-value work

• You anticipate needs before being asked



Key Responsibilities

Executive & Administrative Support

• Manage complex calendars across two executives, including prioritisation

• Draft, edit, and manage communications 
s, memos, presentations)

• Coordinate meetings, agendas, and follow-ups

• Act as a key point of contact across stakeholders



Operations & Project Support

• Support multiple business initiatives (joint and individual)

• Track tasks, deadlines, and deliverables across projects

• Maintain organised systems for documents and workflows

• Improve processes and suggest efficiencies where possible



Financial Administration (No direct handling of funds)

• Track expenses, invoices, and recurring payments

• Assist with account reconciliation and reporting (Xero or similar)

• Maintain accurate financial records and dashboards

• Prepare information for review and approval



Personal & Lifestyle Support

• Travel planning (business and personal)

• Appointment scheduling

• General life admin to support a high-performing household



About You

You are not just organised—you are structured in how you think.



You bring:

• Proven experience as a Virtual Assistant or Executive Assistant (minimum 2–3 years)

• Strong written and verbal English communication

• High attention to detail and reliability

• Ability to manage multiple priorities across different stakeholders

• A proactive mindset—you solve problems before being asked

• Comfort working with ambiguity and bringing clarity

• High level of discretion and professionalism



Tools & Systems Experience

You are comfortable with (or quick to learn):

• Google Workspace / Microsoft Office

• Xero or similar accounting tools

• Project management tools (e.g. Notion, Asana, ClickUp)

• AI tools (e.g. ChatGPT) to improve efficiency



Working Arrangement

• Remote (must be within ±3 hours of AEST / GMT+10)

• Starting at 5–10 hours per week, with potential to scale

• Supporting two executives across shared and individual priorities



Compensation

• $12–18 USD/hour, depending on experience

• Opportunity for increased hours and rate based on performance

• Long-term role for the right candidate



Application Process

To apply, please follow instructions carefully:

1. Start your application with:

“I am the Awesome VA you need”

2. Include only the following:

a. Describe your previous Virtual Assistant / Executive Assistant experience

b. Provide two professional references (company +
—we will verify)

c. What score (1–10) would your previous employer give you, and why?

d. What tools and systems have you used regularly?

e. Describe a time you improved a process or system for your employer

f. Your availability (hours per week and preferred working times)

g. Your earliest start date



Interview Process

1. Application review

2. Video interview (we will assess communication, attention to detail, and work setup)

3. Paid trial period (1 week)



Final Note

We are looking for someone who wants to grow with us long-term.



If you take pride in your work, think ahead, and enjoy bringing order to complexity—we’d like to hear from you.

title: Video Editor - $1,500-2,000+/Month Performance Based Fast-Growing Supplement Brand
description:
We're hiring video editors for Blissta, a supplement brand  is scaling fast. We need editors who can take a script and turn it into a winning ad. Not tomorrow. Today.



**What You'll Do**



Take ad briefs from our creative team and bring them to life. Every script comes with timestamps, visual direction, voiceover, and pacing notes. Your job is to execute it exactly. Every detail matters. The smallest thing can be the difference between an ad that spends $50K and one that gets turned off.



Edit 2+ video ads per day. Source b-roll from YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, stock sites. Find clips that match the script, not random footage. Use AI tools like HeyGen, ElevenLabs, Higgsfield, Flow AI to generate visuals, voiceovers, and animations.



Work directly with our Creative Director. They brief you. You produce. They review. You revise. Nothing goes live until it's perfect.



**Pay Structure**



Starting base: $1,500-2,000/month depending on experience. Performance bonuses kick in week 2. Top editors earn 2,000+ within 60 days based on output and ad performance. If your videos start spending in our ad account your pay goes up immediately. No ceiling. We promote fast. We pay fast.



**What You Need To Know**



How to source footage strategically from YouTube, Pexels, Storyblocks, TikTok, Instagram. How to match visuals to voiceover. Not random b-roll. Intentional clips that match the exact 
nt in the script. Pacing for direct response ads. Fast, punchy, no dead space. Text and caption placement that's readable and scroll-stopping. Basic color correction and audio cleanup. AI tools for video generation, avatar cloning, voiceover. Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, or CapCut.



**What We Provide**



All scripts written for you with full timestamps and visual direction. AI tools for video generation and editing. Stock footage subscriptions. Fast feedback loops so you get better quickly. Long-term work if you're good. A creative team that actually knows what converts.



**Qualifications**



1+ year editing video ads. Not vlogs. Not YouTube videos. Ads. Experience with CapCut, Premiere Pro, or DaVinci Resolve. Experience editing eCommerce or direct response ads. Experience with AI video tools is a huge plus.



**This Is NOT For You If**



You think creative means slow transitions and mood lighting. You can't take feedback. You miss deadlines. You think one video per day is enough. You need hand-holding. You're not hungry.



**This IS For You If**



You're fast and you're hungry. You want to get paid based on what you produce not how many hours you sit at your desk. You understand direct response. You want to work with a team that's scaling something real and you want to grow with it.



**Growth Potential**



Get priority on editing the highest budget ads. Higher pay as you prove yourself. Creative Director path to lead and manage other editors.



**How To Apply**



Send us:

1. Link to 2-3 direct response video ads you've edited. Not brand videos. Not vlogs. Ads.

2. A 2 sentence explanation of why each ad works.

3. Your availability and monthly rate.



Subject line: "Blissta Video Editor — I edit ads that convert"

title: Editing VA -Part-time Capcut / TikTok Editor with potential for more
description:
Part-time Capcut / TikTok Editor with potential for more



Hiring for part-time work for a Capcut / TikTok editor



You will be making TikTok videos using Capcut.  The editing process is fairly simple and straightforward.  



Anyone who is proficient in video editing should have no problem with the basic tasks such as:



Cutting dead space in talking head videos

Adding overlay images on screen in the videos

Minor effects such as dynamic zoom, cropping, etc.

Producing, sorting and compiling b-roll content from voiceover and footage provided, having the ability to digest what is being said and pair up the audio with corresponding footage. These are typically fast cuts, that need to be highly engaging. Lots of movement but simple edits. These will be for Tiktok videos and eventually we will repurpose to other platforms.



Please review a few examples of my content to ensure you are familiar and comfortable with the editing style:



Basic talking head - here you are mostly editing dead space and piecing clips together, adding minor effects like crop and zoom to make the frames look more dynamic and seamless.













Talking head with overlays - same as before, but adding images provided to you on the screen to support what is being said in the video.







B-roll content - you will be provided a voice over and need to work through the footage to find fitting visuals. A strong understanding of English and social media content is crucial here.











Skit work- typically these are one take but a lot of times we need to cut videos together if there are mistakes. You need to be able to recognize those mistakes and clear them out to make it a seamless video. 

















You will be working hand in hand with me on an extremely viral social media page and there is room for bonuses if deadlines are met and the content performs well!



Requirements:

Attention to detail

STRONG English

Strong internet connection

Previous experience with Capcut / TikTok / Instagram

Good working with a team

Ability to learn and take constructive feedback

Availability to work 3-4h a day (with potential for more and increased salary)

Able to work 5-6 days per week (can take days off whenever as long as you are consistent and videos are being completed)

Can take payments through Wise

PH or similar time zone



$300-$400/month + bonuses for 3-4h a day MAX and potential full time work in the future with a salary increase.



To apply, please complete the recreate assignment below so I can see your skills (1h max)



Below is a Google drive with the original files you will need to re-create with a reference video: Please choose 2 



Google drive:

Broll: 

Talking Head: 

Product Video: 





Download the files and import into your video editing software

Please complete the following steps:



Broll: 

Download the files and import into your video editing software

Find the “good” takes of the audio sections and stitch together to complete the voiceover

Find the corresponding footage to match what is being said in the voiceover

Pay close attention to detail in the selection of the clips, the pacing (duration of each scene), cropping, etc.

Message me with a link to the re-created video 



Talking Head: 

Download the files and import into your video editing software

Cut out all dead space between videos

Each clip should flow smoothly into the next with minimal to no pause between words

Tip: cut the clip .2-.3 seconds shorter than the end of the word said.

Do not use transitions between clips, static clips only.

Message me a link to re-create video



Product Video: 

Download the files and import into your video editing software

Cut out all dead space between videos

Each clip should flow smoothly into the next with minimal to no pause between words

Tip: cut the clip .2-.3 seconds shorter than the end of the word said.

Using the reference video and context, place overlays in the proper spots

Pay attention to overlay position and length

Make them big enough to see/read, but not so big they dominate the screen

Do not use any transitions, static overlays only

Message me a link to re-create video





Please Respond back to me beginning with the word “blue”

title: Performance Video Editor for Winning Meta Ads
description:
We’re looking for a highly skilled Performance Video Editor to help us iterate on winning Meta video ads.



This is not a role focused on creating brand films or editing generic social content. Your main job is to take proven winning ad concepts and turn them into high-quality variations that improve engagement, retention, clarity, and freshness, without damaging what made the ad work in the first place.



You will work with existing winning creatives that we hand to you. We will usually provide the strategic direction, testing hypothesis, new hooks when relevant, and structured feedback. We are looking for someone who can execute at a high level, collaborate well, and bring strong performance-editing instincts to the table.



What you’ll do

- Create variations of existing winning Meta video ads

- Improve pacing, tighten edits, and shorten videos where needed

- Add VFX, sound effects, motion emphasis, and visual polish to key moments

- Add and refine subtitles, captions, text overlays, and on-screen hierarchy

- Integrate B-roll and additional voice-over when provided

- Clean up audio and improve overall sound quality

- Clean up cuts and make edits feel sharper and more intentional

- Deliver polished, finished 9:16 exports optimized for Meta paid social

- Revise quickly and accurately based on structured feedback

- Occasionally suggest smart edit improvements beyond the brief, with reasoning



What this role is focused on

This role is about iterating on winners, not reinventing them.



We want someone who understands how to:

- preserve the winning core of an ad

- create controlled variations tied to a specific testing hypothesis

- improve engagement in different ways through editing

- make key moments land harder without overediting

- balance direct-response performance with high production quality



What we’ll provide

- The winning concept to iterate on

- A clear brief and testing hypothesis for each variation

- New hooks when needed

- Ideas for moments to emphasize

- Additional VO, B-roll direction, or text direction when relevant

- Structured feedback for revisions

- Our Brand Book with brand direction



What we need from you

- Strong experience editing performance creative / direct-response ads

- Strong understanding of Meta paid social

- Excellent pacing and retention instincts

- Advanced editing skills beyond basic raw UGC cutting

- Strong subtitle and text-overlay design sense

- Strong VFX / SFX / motion enhancement instincts

- Clean audio editing and finishing ability

- Ability to follow a brief while also contributing thoughtful ideas

- Strong organization, file hygiene, and version control

- Ability to deliver finished work at a consistently high standard



Important constraints

- The first 3 seconds are generally off-limits in terms of changing the underlying footage

- Effects, polish, and editing improvements can be added, but the core opening footage usually stays

- These are hypothesis-driven variations, not random creative remixes

- Revisions are part of the process and should not be an issue

- Deliverables must be high quality, with Meta-optimized resolutions and exports



Ideal profile

You’re likely a strong fit if you:

- have edited for DTC brands or performance agencies

- understand retention, payoff, proof, pacing, and direct-response structure

- know how to make ads feel fresher without changing the core concept too much

- can collaborate with founders / marketers and improve execution quality

- understand that every edit should serve performance, not just aesthetics



Nice to have

- Experience with supplement, wellness, health, or consumer product brands

- Ability to work in Premiere Pro and After Effects

- Ability to source or create tasteful supporting motion / visual enhancement where needed

- Familiarity with version testing workflows



Working setup

- Freelance to start

- Potential for regular ongoing work / retainer if the fit is strong

- Work will be assigned on a concept-by-concept basis around winning ads we want to scale or refresh



To apply

Please send:

- a short introduction

- relevant examples of performance / direct-response ad edits

- examples where you created variations of an existing winning concept

- the software you use

- your typical turnaround times

- your rate structure

- whether you are open to a paid trial task



When applying, include the phrase: “controlled variation beats random editing” so we know you read the full brief.

title: WEB DEVELOPER - WordPress/Elementor
description:
WordPress / Elementor Developer — Freelance 



We’re looking for an experienced WordPress/Elementor developer to build two websites from provided design files. This is a freelance, remote role billed hourly. Candidates based anywhere are welcome, but you must be available for at least a few hours overlap with UK business hours (GMT/BST) for communication and collaboration. 

 

About the project 

The Shala yoga studio and The Shala School of Yoga were once under the same directorship and shared a single website (
). They are now two separate businesses, each with their own director. The shared site needs to be separated into two distinct sites: 

— 
 — for Vahni at The Shala  for  yoga studio classes and workshops 

— The Shala School of Yoga under theshalalondon address, for teacher trainings  

 

The project will be divided into two phases as follows and you will bill the two businesses respectively  

 

PHASE 1 – VAHNI  

What you’ll be doing 

— Archiving the existing site properly so all data is retained and retrievable  

 

— Building a WordPress/Elementor Pro site from provided design files  (Figma/HTML mockups)

 

— Working closely with our team on a clear design handoff and building faithfully to that spec 

 

— Migrating and organising content from the existing shared WordPress site into the new site 

 

— Integrating Momence as the booking platform in the same way it is integrated currently 

 

— Setting up a dynamic events/workshop system with SEO-optimised content in WordPress (not reliant on Momence for indexing) 

 

— Implementing SEO migration — preserving existing URLs, 301 redirects, and applying meta data provided by our SEO team 

 

— Ensuring strong UX throughout — the sites must be clean, fast, accessible, and easy to navigate 

 

PHASE 2 – THE SHALA SCHOOL BUILD 

What you’ll be doing 

— Building a WordPress/Elementor Pro site from provided HTML templates 

 

— Working closely with the team on any updates/design tweaks 

 

— Migrating and organising content from the existing WordPress site into the new site 

 

— Integrating Momence as the booking platform – the integration here is very straightforward. 

 

— Implementing SEO migration — preserving existing URLs, 301 redirects, and applying meta data provided by our SEO team 

 

— Ensuring strong UX throughout — the site must be clean, fast, accessible, and easy to navigate  

 

 

What we’re looking for: 

— Strong WordPress and Elementor Pro experience — you must be proficient, not just familiar. Minimum 5 years experience 

 

— Solid knowledge of WordPress plugins — you should know which tools to use and why 

 

— Comfortable building from a designer’s Figma handoff and/or HTML mockups — your role is to build to spec, not interpret design direction 

 

— Experience with booking platform integrations - we work with Momence

 

— Understanding of SEO best practices in WordPress builds 

 

— Strong UX sensibility — you build sites that feel considered, not just functional 

 

— Clear communicator & excellent English — we’ll be working remotely and need daily progress updates 

 

— Available for at least a few hours overlap with UK business hours 

 

Nice to have 

— Familiarity with The Events Calendar or similar WordPress events plugins 

 

— Experience with WPX hosting 

 

To apply 

Please share  

- a link to your portfolio 

- two or three examples of the best WordPress/Elementor sites you’ve built 

- your hourly rate 

- a brief note on your experience with booking system integrations and working from design handoffs would be helpful. 

 

We’re moving quickly on this — responsive candidates will be prioritised. 



*There may be opportunities for ongoing support and maintenance work following launch.

title: Telecom AutoCAD Permit Drafter (Remote – Telecom Experience Required)
description:
About TESINC, LLC

TESINC, LLC is a fast-growing broadband engineering company specializing in telecom and utility design. We support U.S.-based telecom infrastructure projects and are expanding our remote drafting team.



We are looking for a reliable, experienced Telecom AutoCAD Drafter who wants consistent, long-term work and the opportunity to grow with a strong and supportive team.



Job Overview

You will create permit-ready AutoCAD drawings for telecom infrastructure projects, including fiber networks, utility poles, underground conduits, and small cell deployments.



You’ll collaborate closely with engineers and project managers to produce accurate, high-quality drawings that meet U.S. permitting standards.



Responsibilities

- Create permit-ready AutoCAD drawings for telecom projects

- Develop fiber network layouts, utility pole plans, and underground conduit designs

- Prepare full permit packages for municipalities and utility companies

- Revise drawings based on redlines and feedback

- Ensure compliance with telecom standards and local regulations

- Maintain accuracy, consistency, and attention to detail across all drawings

- Communicate with the team to meet deadlines and project requirements



Required Qualifications

- Strong experience with AutoCAD drafting (telecom or utility focus)

- Experience with fiber, OSP, utility poles, and underground conduit design

- Proven experience creating permit-ready drawings

- Understanding of U.S. telecom permitting processes (or similar)

- Strong skills in AutoCAD (layers, plotting, drafting standards)

- Excellent attention to detail and ability to follow standards

- Strong English communication skills

- Ability to work independently in a remote setting



Nice to Have

- Experience working on U.S. telecom projects

- Background in Civil or Electrical Engineering

- Familiarity with GIS tools (ArcGIS, QGIS)

- Experience with Civil 3D or similar tools



What We Offer

- Long-term, stable work opportunity

- Competitive hourly compensation

- Flexible remote work setup

- Training and onboarding support

- Opportunity to grow with a fast-scaling company

- Collaborative and professional team environment



How to Apply

Please submit:

- Your resume

- Relevant AutoCAD work samples (telecom drawings or permit sets)



In your message, include:

- A brief description of a telecom project you’ve worked on

- Your experience with permit drawings



Send to: 



We’re looking for someone who values quality, consistency, and long-term collaboration. If you’re experienced in telecom drafting and want a reliable, ongoing opportunity with a growing team, we’d love to work with you.
