ABOUT US



MySweetSmile is an award-winning teeth whitening and oral care brand with over 1,000,000 customers globally. We're the UK's #1 teeth whitening brand (The Independent, 2023 & 2024), sold across Amazon UK, US, and EU, Shopify, TikTok Shop, and multiple retail marketplaces. We're growing fast and need someone to own our marketplace operations full-time.



THE ROLE



You'll be the person who lives inside our Amazon accounts and marketplace platforms every single day. Your job is to ensure every listing, every market, and every backend detail is optimised, accurate, and up to date — so the founder doesn't have to.

This is not a VA role. This is full ownership of marketplace operations across multiple platforms and countries.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN



Amazon (Primary — 50% of role)

- Listing management across UK, US, DE, FR, ES, IT, NL, SE, PL — titles, bullet points, descriptions, backend keywords, search terms, product categories

- A+ Content creation, Brand Story updates, and image gallery management

- Flat file uploads and feed file management

- Listing experiments (A/B testing titles, images, bullets)

- Promotions setup and management (coupons, deals, Brand Tailored Promotions)

- Backend compliance: ingredients, directions for use, safety warnings, category accuracy

- Translations and localisation for EU marketplaces (using AI tools is expected)

- Keeping all marketplaces aligned to a universal standard set by the founder



New Product Launches & Marketplace Expansion (15% of role)

- End-to-end listing creation for new MySweetSmile products across all active marketplaces — content, A+ pages, compliance, and go-live readiness

- Facilitating new marketplace launches as we expand into UAE, Saudi Arabia, Japan, Singapore, and beyond — account setup, listing creation, marketplace-specific requirements, and ensuring all content meets local standards

- Working with the founder and operations team to coordinate launch timelines



Other Marketplaces (10% of role)

- TikTok Shop listing & store management

- Superdrug, Debenhams, OnBuy, and other retail marketplace listings

- Ensuring product data, imagery, and pricing is consistent across all channels



Inventory & Stock Coordination (10% of role)

- Monitoring FBA stock levels daily across all marketplaces

- Flagging restock requirements to the operations team before stockouts occur

- Coordinating on inbound shipment timelines and inventory planning

- Tracking stock positions across non-Amazon channels



Reporting & Competitor Monitoring (10% of role)

- Weekly marketplace performance report summarising sales, stock levels, and key metrics across all channels

- Reviewing advertising agency performance and flagging concerns to the founder

- Weekly competitor monitoring — tracking pricing, listing changes, and new product activity in our category



Creative & Design (5% of role)

- A+ Content design and image gallery creation in Canva

- Updating lifestyle and product imagery across listings

- Graphic design for marketplace-specific assets



WHAT YOU MUST HAVE — NON-NEGOTIABLE



If you don't have all of the following, this role is not for you.



- 3+ years of hands-on Amazon Seller Central experience — you know flat files, backend fields, feed management, and catalogue operations inside out. Not just UI editing.

- Experience managing listings across at least 5 Amazon marketplaces

- Experience launching products on new Amazon marketplaces (especially UAE, JP, SA, SG, AU)

- Confident with A+ Content, Brand Registry, and Brand Story creation

- Confident with Canva or similar design tools for A+ content, image galleries, and basic graphic design

- Experience with TikTok Shop or other non-Amazon marketplace platforms

- Experience with Shopify (product page updates, imagery, descriptions, pricing)

- Experience with Helium 10, Jungle Scout, or similar Amazon tools

- Basic understanding of Amazon PPC — enough to review an agency's performance, flag issues, and ask the right questions

- Experience with European marketplace translations and localisation

- Experience using AI tools (Claude, etc.) for copywriting, translations, and productivity — this is how we work and you'll be expected to use them daily

- Amazon compliance knowledge: ingredients, origin documentation, safety warnings, category requirements

- Strong written English

- Self-starter who takes full ownership and doesn't wait to be told what to do

- Detail-obsessed — you spot a wrong bullet point, a missing keyword, or an incorrect category and it bothers you



TOOLS WE USE

-Amazon Seller Central (global), Canva, Shopify, Notion, Slack, Google Sheets, Google Drive, Helium 10, AI tools (Claude)



SCHEDULE



Flexible within UK business hours overlap. We need at least 4–5 hours of crossover with UK time (GMT/BST) daily. Full-time, 50 hours per week, 6 days (1 fixed day off).



COMPENSATION



Depending on experience. Long-term role with room to grow as the brand scales into new markets and product lines.



THIS ROLE IS NOT FOR YOU IF:



- You only know basic VA-level Amazon tasks

- You've never worked with flat files or feed management

- You don't have marketplace expansion experience

- You can't design A+ content or work in Canva

- You need hand-holding or frequent check-ins

- You've never used AI tools for productivity

- You can't work independently across time zones

- You want a regular 9-5. 



HOW TO APPLY



Title your application: "MSS Amazon & Ecommerce Manager — [Your Name]"



Include:

- A short intro (3–4 sentences) on why you're the right fit

- A link to a Google Sheet or Doc showing your Amazon marketplace experience — specifically which marketplaces you've managed, what tasks you handled, and any results you achieved

- One example of A+ Content or an Amazon listing you've optimised (screenshot or link)



Applications that don't follow these specific steps will not be reviewed.

title: Video Editor who #Believes in Entrepreneurs| Flexible Work Schedule
description:
We're trying to solve the world's biggest problem: People don't #Believe in themselves enough. For over 22 years, Evan Carmichael has been dedicated to this mission. The way we're approaching this problem is producing inspirational content at scale. The right video at the right time can completely change someone's life and we want to touch as many lives as possible. People may not have a supportive family or community around them, but our content can help give them the belief they need to chase their dreams and make the world a better place. We want to work with people who also want to make an impact and touch people's lives. In your application, please put ‘I am your next awesome Video Editor!’ in the subject line. Please apply through OnlineJobsPh and not via
. The job responsibilities include editing the first minute of videos to grab and keep the audience's attention, selecting the most engaging clips, adding eye-catching transitions and effects, ensuring the sound and visuals are top-notch, and staying on top of the latest trends to keep things fresh. Our goal is to get to 70% audience retention in the first minute. Additionally, strong creativity, attention to detail, and effective communication skills are essential to produce high-quality videos that engage viewers and meet retention goals. Please also share the link to your resume and portfolio, what your hourly salary expectations are, and in the first line of your message tell us how many YouTube subscribers Evan Carmichael has, followed by who your favorite entrepreneur of all time is and why. The job is full time (40 hours per week) with a flexible schedule. We would start off with a small project for now and we'd pay you for your ideas and research. We also like to work with people over a long period of time, not just short contracts, and give them opportunities to succeed and do more based on what they want to learn. Hopefully that will be you! Thank you for reading and good luck! Sending lots of love! -Irish #Believe

title: VIDEO EDITOR
description:
Before you apply, read this carefully.

We run one of the fastest-growing pet brands online.

DTC. Performance-focused. High volume.

And we're looking for a Video Editor who actually knows what they're doing.

Not someone who cuts clips together.

Someone who makes people stop scrolling.

What the job is:

You receive a script. You deliver a scroll-stopping, emotion-driven video ad. Within 24 hours.

That's it. 

What we need from you:

? Strong editing skills (pacing, sound design, motion, storytelling)

? AI tools fluency : you must be actively using AI in your workflow (voiceover, generation, enhancement,.. we don't care which, but you need to know them)

? Consistency. Speed. No excuses.

? Available for weekly projects, long-term

You are NOT a fit if:

? You apply with a generic message

? You've never edited a performance ad before

? You need 3 days to deliver a 60-second video

? You don't use AI tools in your process



The Hiring Process:



Trial Task : 45 to 60 sec video from our script. 24h delivery. Unpaid. This filters out everyone who's not serious.

Paid Project Trial : Full brand video. If it's good, short interview follows.

One-Month Paid Trial : Weekly projects with feedback.

Long-Term Offer : For those who consistently deliver.





ONE MORE THING.

We get a lot of applications.

Most of them are copy-paste. Vague. Useless.

If yours looks like that, we won't even open it.

To apply, your message must follow this exact structure :



Full Name:

Age:

City:

Years of experience in video editing:

AI tools you actively use:

Link to past video ads (not general reels, ad content):

One sentence: why should we pick you over everyone else?

Loom Video (2-3 min, in English, introduce yourself):

If your message doesn't follow this structure exactly, it gets ignored.

We're not harsh. We're just focused.

The right person will love this.

title: Long-Form Video Editor (Agency Full Time Job)
description:
About Us

We’re The 1% Studio — a fast-growing editing agency that helps creators, entrepreneurs, and brands scale their online presence through high-quality content. We specialize in creating high-performing YouTube videos, Reels, and TikToks that drive growth, engagement, and authority.



About the Role

We’re looking for a highly skilled Long-Form YouTube Editor to join our team and create high-quality trading education content. This is not basic editing — we want someone who can bring concepts to life through clean visuals, strong storytelling, and advanced motion graphics.



You’ll be editing in-depth trading videos that break down strategies, concepts, and market analysis in a way that is both engaging and easy to understand. The goal is to turn complex information into visually compelling, high-retention content.



You must be able to edit at a very high level, similar to top creators in the finance and education space, with a strong focus on motion graphics, animation, and clean visual storytelling.



You’ll need to be able to edit videos like this:







Responsibilities



Edit long-form YouTube videos (8–20 minutes) focused on trading education

Use advanced motion graphics and animation to simplify and explain trading concepts

Create engaging intros (30–60 seconds) using the strongest hooks, visuals, and key 
nts

Design clean, high-quality on-screen visuals (charts, diagrams, overlays, annotations)

Maintain a consistent, premium visual style across all content

Structure videos for maximum retention, pacing, and clarity

Communicate clearly, take feedback well, and deliver edits on time



Requirements



Expert-level video editing skills (Premiere Pro / Final Cut / After Effects)

Strong motion graphics and animation ability (this is essential)

Experience editing educational or finance-related content is a big plus

Deep understanding of pacing, storytelling, and viewer retention

Ability to turn complex topics into simple, engaging visuals

Strong attention to detail in sound design, color grading, and overall polish

title: Skilled YouTube Editor (Finance / Trading Content)

title: Real Estate Virtual Assistant (Long-Term) — Indiana Real Estate Business
description:
About the Role



I am a real estate agent based in Indiana, building a high-quality real estate business in the Indianapolis Northside market (Carmel, Fishers, Westfield, Zionsville, Noblesville, Greenwood, and Franklin).



The "German" Advantage



Originally from Germany, I run my business with a focus on structure, clarity, precision, and reliability.



I am not looking for a short-term freelancer — I am looking for a long-term partner.



In my previous business, my Virtual Assistant worked with me for over 7 years. I value loyalty, consistency, and professionalism, and I offer a stable, respectful, and long-term working environment in return.



You will work directly with me and play a key role in building this business from the ground up.



Work Schedule & Compensation

Working Hours: Flexible, with 3-hour overlap with US Eastern Time (e.g., 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM EST)

Payment: Reliable, consistent, and on-time payments

Growth: Opportunity to grow as the business grows



Your Growth Path (Phased Responsibilities)

Phase 1: Immediate Priority (Open House & Outreach)



• Contact listing agents to schedule open house opportunities

• Track open house opportunities

• Follow up with listing agents

• Maintain open house tracking list

• Prepare open house materials

• Prepare follow-up templates

• Prepare open house reports

Listing Support

• Track listing activity



Buyer Support

• Track contract deadlines

• Prepare buyer materials



Lead Generation & Farming



• Farming area research

• Database building

• Contact research

• Pipeline tracking

• CRM management



Administrative Support



•
 management

• Calendar coordination

• Appointment scheduling

• Data entry

• CRM maintenance



Your Profile



• Experience as a Real Estate Virtual Assistant (preferred)

• Highly organized and detail-oriented

• Reliable and proactive

• Comfortable working independently

• Excellent English communication skills

• Looking for long-term collaboration



Tools & Systems



You do not need experience with all tools — training will be provided.



CRM: Cloze / Moxi Engage

MLS: MIBOR / BLC

Transaction: Dotloop / SkySlope / DocuSign

Coordination: BrokerBay / ShowingTime / Supra

Marketing: MoxiPresent / MoxiImpress / Canva



Why This Role Is a Great Opportunity



• Long-term collaboration

• Direct work with business owner

• Build a business from the ground up

• Growing responsibilities over time

• Potential full-time role



I value clear communication, reliability, and a positive working relationship.



How to Apply



To ensure attention to detail, please follow these instructions:

Provide a short introduction about yourself

Describe your real estate experience

List tools you have used

Confirm your availability for US Eastern Time overlap

Share your hourly rate

Provide examples of previous work (if available)



I look forward to building a long-term partnership.



Frank

title: Hero Wanted – Student Housing Operations & Leasing Coordinator
description:
TL;DR – Role Summary



Founder-led student housing company looking for a detail-obsessed, judgment-driven property manager. This is a long-term role built on trust, ownership, and clear communication.



You are going to be the HERO in what we do here — we offer reliable, modern student living for college students in the U.S. We have properties in the states of California, New York, and Arizona.







WHY work with us



We are a small and nimble team. You will be the first few overseas employees we hire. Imagine being  founding tea
mbers joining early: you will get to learn, grow, and help shape how things are done as we scale.



You will work with people who are humble, considerate, and open to feedback. You will be an important part of the team as we provide reliable housing for engineering college students who are working extremely hard in school.



If you have a great idea, pitch it. We value feedback and believe we all grow faster through suggestions and shared thinking.



If this first phase of training and responsibilities goes well, we plan to expand into other businesses later, with the goal to grow into 20–40 hours per week over time.



**Note: this is an individual contributor role to start. ‘Manager’ refers to managing properties, processes, and responsibilities — not managing people, yet.







HOURS



Training/ Probation: 

First 1-2 months, Monday - Friday, 9:30am - 12pm EST, totally 10-20 hours a week (actual hours subject to small variations). 



Official: 

Monday–Friday: 10am - 5pm EST (35 hrs) + 6:30-8:30pm EST ONE evening (2 hrs; March - June only, actual day to be determined, based on future Open House hours): 

Available to answer call ASAP, and respond to text messages <30 min. Totally 40 hours a week.



Saturday, Sunday: Anytime of your choice, 1-1.5 hr EACH Day: Respond to urgent text messages and possible emergency maintenance calls.



The nature of property management work can fluctuate throughout the year. Peak periods include leasing season (March–June) and move-in/move-out season (May–August), while the remaining months are typically lighter. During slower periods, you may be assigned additional tasks or responsibilities based on overall business needs.







SALARY



SALARY $3–$6/hr. We believe in happy, fairly compensated employees.



Please share:



How much you would like to earn per hour to start

Where do you see yourself professionally in 1–3 years, and what does growth look like to you?



Please be realistic — the higher your ask, the higher our performance expectations will be.

title: Short-Form Content Clipper & Distribution Specialist (Part-Time)
description:
I’m looking for a skilled, reliable, and self-motivated virtual assistant to help me build and operate a short-form content distribution system for my YouTube ministry channel, Prophetic Pigeons.

This is NOT a generic social media management role. You will be building a multi-account distribution network from scratch — creating and managing 40+ themed social media accounts across TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, and X 
), then clipping and distributing short-form video content across all of them on a daily basis.

Your job has two content tracks:

Track 1 — Curated Content (Other Creators)

You will identify, clip, and post viral content from well-known faith and prophetic creators across the space. This is how we grow the accounts — by curating the best content in the niche so each themed page builds a real, engaged following. You will always credit original creators appropriately.

Track 2 — Original Content (Prophetic Pigeons)

You will clip my long-form YouTube teachings, sermons, Bible studies, and prophetic content into short-form videos (15–90 seconds) optimized for each platform. These clips get mixed into the account feeds alongside the curated content at a strategic ratio.



The goal: build 40+ active themed pages that collectively generate hundreds of thousands of views per month, driving attention and followers back to the main Prophetic Pigeons channel.



Key Responsibilities

Account Creation & Growth

 ? Create and warm 40+ niche-themed accounts across TikTok, Instagram, YouTube Shorts, and X

 ? Follow proper account warming protocols (7–10 days of organic engagement before posting)

 ? Develop unique bios, profile images, and niche angles for each account

 ? Manage cross-account engagement (comments, duets/stitches, story shares)

Competitive Research & Curation

 ? Identify the top 15–20 viral creators in the faith, prophetic, Bible teaching, and worship space

 ? Monitor trending clips weekly and pull top performers into the content rotation

 ? Track which content formats, hooks, and topics perform best across platforms

 ? Build and maintain a clip source library organized by creator, topic, and platform

Clip Production

 ? Clip long-form Prophetic Pigeons videos into 15–25 short-form pieces per video

 ? Clip viral moments from other established creators in the faith space

 ? Add captions (auto-generated + reviewed for accuracy), hook text overlays, and branded outro frames

 ? Create 2–3 hook variations per clip for multi-account distribution

 ? Produce 80–100+ clips per month across both content tracks

Distribution & Scheduling

 ? Post 2–3x per day per account (mix of curated + original content)

 ? Stagger posting times across accounts to avoid platform spam detection

 ? Maintain a content calendar and posting schedule

 ? Target 2,000–3,000+ total posts per month across the network by Month 2

Analytics & Reporting

 ? Track follower growth, views, and engagement per account weekly

 ? Identify top-performing clips, hooks, formats, and posting times

 ? Deliver a simple weekly analytics report

 ? Recommend strategy adjustments based on data

Systems & Documentation (Month 3)

 ? Document all workflows into a comprehensive SOP package

 ? Create brand guidelines, clip templates, and approved content examples

 ? Set up and build out a Whop community for external clippers

 ? Write onboarding materials and submission guidelines for clippers

 ? Assist in recruiting and onboarding the first 5–8 clippers



Required Skills

 ? Strong experience with short-form video editing — CapCut proficiency is essential

 ? Deep familiarity with TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, and X — you understand how the algorithms work, what content gets pushed, and how to grow pages

 ? Experience creating or managing theme pages / fan pages / clip accounts — this is the #1 qualification. If you’ve built niche pages before, you understand the game.

 ? Eye for viral content — you can watch a 30-minute video and know which 45 seconds will get the most engagement

 ? Self-motivated and organized — this is a part-time role with a lot of autonomy. You need to manage your own schedule and hit daily posting targets without being micromanaged.

 ? Comfortable working with faith-based / Christian content — you don’t have to share the faith, but you must be respectful of the content and understand the audience

 ? Strong written English — for captions, bios, and clipper community management

title: Brand Deal Fulfillment Coordinator
description:
We broker and manage brand partnerships on behalf of our creators — handling negotiations, deal structure, and execution so our talent can focus on making content. This role sits right at the center of that work.



We're looking for someone to own the execution side of our brand deals from close to delivery. You'll be the bridge between our brand clients and our talent — making sure creators are briefed, concepts get executed correctly, timelines are hit, and every deal lands cleanly.



From day one you'll be in direct contact with brands and talent on real, active campaigns. This is a coordination and accountability role, not a creative or strategy role. If you get satisfaction from making sure nothing falls through the cracks — and you want to learn the influencer business from the inside — this is the right seat.



—



HOW A DEAL MOVES THROUGH YOU



Receive & review the concept — Review deal terms and the creative brief. Make sure deliverables, usage rights, and deadlines are airtight before anything moves to the creator.

Brief the creator — Deliver the brief, confirm they understand expectations, and answer questions. You're the primary point of contact between the brand and talent during production.

Manage production & approvals — Track submissions, run the brand review process, and coordinate any revision requests or reshoots until the concept is signed off.

Confirm go-live — Verify content posted on schedule and all deliverable requirements were met.

Close it out — Alert accounting to send the invoice. Flag the completed deal to your manager so they can go after the next one.

—



QUALIFICATIONS



Required: · Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field · Strong written and verbal communication skills · Highly organized — able to manage multiple active deals simultaneously · Comfortable holding people to deadlines without burning the relationship · Detail-oriented — you catch when a submission doesn't match the brief



Preferred: · Prior experience in account management, talent coordination, PR, or agency operations · Familiarity with influencer marketing platforms (Grin, Aspire, or similar) · Working knowledge of major social platforms and how sponsorship disclosures work



—



THIS ROLE DOES NOT INCLUDE · Market research or trend analysis · Sourcing or pitching brand deals · Creative strategy or campaign concepting · Content creation or social media management



—





WHAT IT'S LIKE HERE



We care about output, not hours logged.



If you're early in your career, hungry to learn, and want to be close to where deals actually get made — we want to hear from you.



To be considered, please let respond by telling us the following in your response

1. Have you worked a deal fulfillment role before?

2. Are you someone who is extremely responsive on a moments notice?

3. What is your favorite animal and why?

title: Designer & Web Developer – Automation Platform (MVP/POC)
description: We are looking for a talented and versatile Designer & Web Developer to join our team in building the first version of an automation-driven platform from the ground up. You will be responsible for crafting intuitive, visually compelling interfaces that make complex automation workflows feel simple and accessible to everyday users. This role blends creative design thinking with solid front-end development skills, requiring proficiency in tools like Figma for design and React or similar frameworks for implementation. You will collaborate closely with our product and automation engineering team to translate business requirements into clean, functional, and scalable UI components. The initial scope focuses on delivering a polished MVP/POC, but this engagement is intended to grow into a long-term collaboration as the platform evolves. A strong eye for UX is essential, as you will be designing user flows, dashboards, and interaction patterns that simplify automation management for non-technical users. Experience integrating front-end interfaces with REST APIs or automation platforms such as Zapier, Make, or custom-built pipelines is a strong advantage. We value someone who is proactive, communicates clearly, and can work independently while aligning with a small, fast-moving team. The ideal candidate is comfortable with ambiguity in early-stage product development and excited by the opportunity to shape a product from its foundation. If you are a creative problem-solver who loves building meaningful digital experiences and wants to grow with a product long-term, we would love to connect.

title: Senior Liaison Officer - Remote
description:
We are a U.S.-based investment firm focusing on risk monitoring and management for prediction purpose. We specialize in watching geopolitical hot topics, providing updates in advance in order to prove the stable profits growth of clients we serve for. In different regions around the world, focuses vary, and we tailor our services to meet specific needs. Given to the geographical location and strategic importance, the Philippines has always been a hot hub around the Indo-Pacific region.



In such context, we are now seeking a Senior Liaison Officer for our Philippine office. This role will serve as a critical bridge between our clients, external stakeholders, and internal teams. The officer will focus on building networks, ensuring smooth information flow, and aligning client needs with strategic and practical goals.



Key Responsibilities:

•Information Gathering: Collect relevant information from government agencies, legislative bodies, and other stakeholders to support clients' policy analysis and decision-making.

•Report Writing: Prepare clear, concise, and well-organized reports based on on-site information collection, tailored to project requirements for clients' review.

•Risk Monitoring: Track emerging risks and provide timely warnings about potential changes that may affect the smooth operation of the tasks .



Qualifications:

•Prior employment or direct experience within key national governments, departments, and the Congress

•In-depth understanding of Philippine foreign policy, executive and intergovernmental communication channels, or other related topics

•Familiarity with national development plans, foreign policy priorities, and interagency coordination mechanisms

•Bachelor's degree or higher in Political Science, Public Administration, Law, International Relations, or related disciplines

•Demonstrated professionalism, discretion, and political sensitivity

•Ability to work independently, remotely, and with flexible hours

•Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English

, WhatsApp, and other sources

You will need:

title: Meta Ads Creative Designer (Facebook & Instagram)
description:
Meta Ads Creative Designer – Acoustia (Part-time, with fast-track to Full-time)

Acoustia is a fast-growing Dutch e-commerce brand selling affordable, discrete in-ear hearing aids to customers in the Netherlands and Belgium. We sell directly online and advertise aggressively on
 and Instagram to reach our target audience — mostly seniors and their families who are looking for a high-quality hearing solution at a fair price.

We are looking for a talented Creative Designer to join our team and produce high-performing Meta ad creatives. You will start part-time, and if the quality of your work is strong, we will move to full-time quickly.

What you'll be making:

You will be producing around 10–15 creatives per day to start, increasing over time. This includes static image ads, video ads, reels, carousels, and stories — all in Dutch, as we advertise in the Netherlands and Belgium. If you specialize specifically in statics or video, you are still very welcome to apply.

How we work:

We move fast. We will provide you with the strategy behind each creative, and ad copy when needed. We will also share references from competitors so you always have clear direction. In return, we expect you to meet deadlines without being reminded — we advertise at high speed and timing matters.

What we're looking for:



Strong eye for design and the ability to make creatives that stop the scroll

Any tools are fine — we use Canva Pro, Claude and Higgsfield ourselves, but what matters is the quality of the output

Proactive communication — don't wait for us to follow up, keep us in the loop

Open to feedback and revisions without taking it personally

Fluent in English for all communication with our team (the creatives themselves will be in Dutch)



Portfolio requirement:

We will not consider any application that does not include a portfolio or examples of Meta ad creatives you have previously made. Please include a link to your work or attach samples directly. This is non-negotiable.

title: Experienced Credit Repair Disputer (FCRA & FDCPA Expert)
description:
Experienced Credit Repair Disputer (FCRA & FDCPA Expert)



**Job Description:**



We are looking for a **skilled, proactive, and highly organized Credit Repair Disputer** to join our growing credit repair team. The ideal candidate must have **strong experience with U.S. credit laws, dispute strategies, and credit bureau processes**.



This role requires someone who is detail-oriented, knowledgeable, and capable of handling disputes professionally while maintaining accurate documentation for every client file.



**Responsibilities:**



* Review client credit reports from all three bureaus (Experian, Equifax, TransUnion)

* Identify **inaccuracies, inconsistencies, and violations**

* Write **personalized dispute letters** for each client and each dispute round

* Include **screenshots of the credit report highlighting the specific inaccuracies** being challenged

* Track disputes and responses from credit bureaus and creditors

* **Rewrite and refresh dispute letters every 90 days** to ensure they pass through the **ACDV system used by credit bureaus**

* Maintain organized client files and dispute history

* Communicate professionally with the team regarding dispute progress

* Follow **FCRA and FDCPA compliance standards**



**Requirements:**



* Strong knowledge of **FCRA and FDCPA laws**

* Proven experience in **credit repair dispute processing**

* Ability to write **custom dispute letters (not templates)**

* Understanding of how the **ACDV system works**

* Excellent **organization and attention to detail**

* Ability to **highlight and document inaccuracies with screenshots**

* Strong written English skills

* Professional, reliable, and easy to work with



**Preferred Experience:**



* Experience working with **credit repair companies**

* Familiarity with **Metro 2 reporting**

* Knowledge of common dispute strategies and compliance practices



**How to Apply:**



Please include:



1. Your **experience with credit repair disputes**

2. Which **laws and dispute strategies you typically use**

3. A **sample dispute letter you have written**

4. The credit repair software you have used (if any)



We are looking for someone **long-term who takes pride in doing detailed, high-quality dispute work.**

title: AI Voice Developer - Production-Ready Voice Agents & SMS Automation
description:
We're ToneHaus, an AI voice agency building custom voice AI systems for trades and SMBs. We're looking for a production-ready AI voice developer to build out 2 voice AI systems and 1 SMS automation agent starting Monday.



This is not entry-level. We need someone with proven experience shipping production voice agents, not proof of concepts or tutorials.



THE PROJECT

Phase 1 (Weeks 2-3):



- Build 2 custom voice AI agents (inbound call handling, lead qualification, appointment booking)

- Build 1 SMS automation system (lead follow-up, confirmations, notifications)

- Integration with existing CRM/booking platforms (Payaca, BigChange and ServiceM8)

- Testing and refinement



Phase 2 (Ongoing):

If Phase 1 goes well, we have a consistent pipeline of client projects through our agency. This could be 20-40 hours/week of ongoing work with long-term stability.



WE NEED

? Proven experience building production voice AI agents (not tutorials, not demos)

? Evidence of work: active phone number from a past client, backend system walkthrough, case study, or similar

? Experience with voice/telephony platforms (11Labs, Retell AI, Twilio, etc.)

? Understanding of LLMs and prompt engineering

? Experience with n8n or similar automation platforms

? Ability to integrate with CRM/booking systems (Calendly, HubSpot, Airtable, etc.)

? Reliable communication and organised workflow



WHAT WE'LL ASK IN YOUR APPLICATION

Before applying, be prepared to answer:



Your Voice AI Experience: Walk us through a production system you built. What was the business problem? What tools did you use? What were the results?



Your Evidence: Provide one of the following:



An active phone number from a past client's business (we'll call it to test)

A backend system walkthrough (Loom video or call) showing your architecture

A detailed case study with metrics

Links to deployed systems you've built



Your Building Process: 



- How do you approach building a new voice AI system? Walk us through your workflow from requirements to deployment.



- Testing & Quality Assurance: How do you test voice agents? What does your QA process look like? How do you catch edge cases?



- Your Tech Stack: What LLMs do you use? (Claude, GPT-4, etc.) What voice platforms? What do you use for automation and integration? Why those choices?



- Communication & Organisation: How do you communicate progress? What tools do you use to stay organised? How do you handle revisions and feedback?



- Your Experience: Years building voice AI? What types of businesses have you worked with? What's the most complex system you've built?





WHY THIS MATTERS

We work with real businesses: plumbers, HVAC contractors, med spas, roofing companies. Their customers are calling expecting a real experience. We need someone who:



- Ships production-quality code

- Tests thoroughly

- Thinks about edge cases and user experience

- Communicates clearly and promptly

- Can scale with us



WHAT'S NEXT?



If you have solid production experience and real evidence of work, apply with:



- A brief intro

- Answers to the 7 questions above

- Your evidence (link, phone number, or Loom video)



We'll review applications and start conversations with people who meet the requirements.

ntum as a company and get new clients at a fast rate, so right now, we're hiring both contractors (Per Job Basis) and Potential full-time editors to add to our amazing media team.

dia

title: Blog Writer and Pinterest Copywriter for Gift Guide Website
description:
Hi! I'm Mimi, and I run a growing gift guide website that helps people find thoughtful, unique gifts for every occasion. I'm looking for a talented writer to join our small team and help bring this site to life with high-quality gift guide articles and Pinterest copy.



What you'll be doing:

Writing complete gift guide articles (think "Best Gifts for Beach Lovers" or "Unique White Elephant Gifts Under $30") from research through final draft. This includes finding and curating the best products for each guide, writing engaging product descriptions and article copy, writing Pinterest pin titles and descriptions optimized for search, and basic on-page SEO (keywords, meta descriptions, headings).



All content is ghostwritten under the brand's voice and style. You'll be working from article briefs and following our style guide, but I'm looking for someone who can own the full writing process and deliver articles that need only light editing from me before publishing.



You'd be a great fit if you:

Have strong English writing skills with a warm, conversational tone (think talking to a friend, not writing a textbook). Have experience writing product roundups, gift guides, or affiliate content. Understand basic SEO and know how to naturally work keywords into content. Are comfortable doing product research across sites like Amazon, Etsy, and brand websites. Have some familiarity with Pinterest SEO and writing pin descriptions that rank. Are detail-oriented and can follow a style guide consistently. Can work independently and meet deadlines without a lot of hand-holding.



Bonus points if you:

Have experience with WordPress. Have written for lifestyle, gift, or shopping content sites before. Are genuinely interested in gift-giving and love finding cool products.



Benefits and details:

Looking for someone who can work full-time hours. The start date is as soon as possible. You'll get paid with PayPal. The training period is 10 work days. This is your trial period. You'll get 50% pay on day 1 and 50% on day 10. After day 10, you'll be paid once a month. 13th month pay (prorated for your first year). 2 weeks of paid time off per calendar year. 2 weeks of unpaid time off. Time off and paid for all national holidays.



Do you want to work from home with flexible hours? Are you a strong writer who loves finding the perfect gift for people? Do you want a steady role with a small, growing team?



If that sounds like you, apply via this form and tell me what the last great gift you gave someone was (and why you picked it): 



Applications directly via OnlineJobs are ignored.



Thanks so much!

Mimi

California, USA

title: Growth Content & UGC Manager (AI-Powered Social + Video)
description:
About Us

MyBellaBaby is a fast-growing Canadian baby brand providing free baby samples and weekly giveaways to expecting and new parents.



We are scaling our organic and paid content and need a Growth Content Manager who can oversee:



• AI-assisted content creation

• Daily social media posting

• UGC sourcing & coordination

• Short-form video production

• Repurposing long-form content

• Performance-driven iteration



This is not just a posting role.



This is a growth-focused position.



Role Overview

You will manage and execute our entire content engine across:



• TikTok

• Instagram

•

• YouTube (Shorts + Long Form)



You will use AI tools, editing software, and performance insights to produce high-quality content consistently (2–3 posts per day across platforms).



The goal is growth, engagement, and lead generation.



Core Responsibilities

1?? AI Content Creation & Scheduling

• Generate 60–90 posts per month using AI tools (ChatGPT, etc.)

• Create captions, hooks, and CTA variations

• Design simple branded graphics in Canva

• Upload and schedule via Metricool (or similar platform)

• Ensure content aligns with monthly strategy themes



2?? Video Production & Repurposing

• Produce 10–20 short-form videos per month

• Edit UGC clips into high-performing ad-style videos

• Repurpose long-form YouTube content into multiple shorts

• Add captions and motion text

• Deliver optimized formats (9:16, 16:9, etc.)



3?? UGC Management

• Identify and coordinate micro-influencers or moms

• Provide them with creative briefs

• Collect and organize raw footage

• Edit UGC into ads and organic posts

• Manage simple release permissions



4?? Performance & Optimization

• Review weekly performance metrics

• Identify top-performing hooks and themes

• Create variations of winning content

• Provide short weekly reports with insights

• Suggest new creative angles monthly



Required Skills

• 3+ years experience managing social media growth

• Strong video editing skills (CapCut, Premiere, Final Cut, Descript)

• Experience using AI tools for content creation

• Understanding of paid social ad creative

• Experience repurposing content efficiently

• Ability to manage UGC creators

• Strong English writing skills

• Organized and deadline-driven



Tools We Expect You to Be Comfortable With

• ChatGPT

• Canva Pro

• CapCut / Premiere / Descript

• ElevenLabs (optional)

• Metricool / Buffer / similar scheduler

• Google Drive

• Basic analytics review (Meta / TikTok / YouTube dashboards)



Deliverables (Monthly)

• 60+ scheduled posts across platforms

• 10–20 short-form videos

• 2–4 longer informational YouTube videos

• 8–12 UGC-based testimonial clips

• Organized content calendar

• Weekly performance summary



Success Metrics

• Increased engagement rate

• Increased video retention

• Increased click-through to landing page

• Lower cost per lead (for paid creatives)

• Consistent content output without founder involvement



Compensation based on skill level and output.



How to Apply

Please include:



Portfolio links (social accounts or videos managed)

Tools you use and your workflow

Experience with UGC coordination

Example of a 15-second hook idea for Canadian moms promoting a free baby box

Monthly compensation expectation

Availability (hours per week + timezone)

Applications without portfolio examples will not be reviewed.



Hiring Process

• Shortlist review

• Paid test project

• 30-day trial

• Ongoing contract

title: E-Commerce Inventory & Production Manager (Shopify)
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE





We are a fast-growing, multi-million dollar women’s fashion brand looking for an experienced Inventory & Production Manager to take ownership of our inventory forecasting, restocks, and production coordination.







This role is critical to the business. You will be responsible for ensuring our top-selling products stay in stock, production runs smoothly, and inventory decisions are made based on data — not guesswork.







This role requires someone who can independently manage inventory forecasting and production timelines. This is not a training role.



COMPENSATION





• Starting at $1,800/month (full-time)



• Opportunity for growth based on performance



• Long-term role with a growing global brand



CORE RESPONSIBILITIES





• Manage inventory forecasting using Inventory Planner (or similar tools)



• Monitor sell-through rates and product performance weekly



• Identify restock needs BEFORE products sell out







• Create and manage purchase orders with manufacturers (China & Turkey)



• Confirm ALL production orders before opening any pre-orders



• Track production timelines and follow up with suppliers consistently







• Ensure accurate ETAs are communicated internally



• Prevent overselling or selling inventory that has not been confirmed







• Generate weekly top-seller reports for the marketing and ads team



• Clearly communicate which products should be prioritized for scaling







• Upload and manage products in Shopify including:



– Inventory quantities



– Product naming



– SEO-friendly product descriptions







• Maintain a clean, accurate, and organized Shopify backend



REQUIREMENTS (NON-NEGOTIABLE)





• Proven experience managing inventory for an e-commerce brand



• Strong understanding of inventory forecasting and sell-through analysis



• Experience using Inventory Planner or similar forecasting tools







• Experience working with overseas manufacturers and production timelines



• Strong understanding of Shopify backend







• Ability to work independently and make data-driven decisions



• High attention to detail with minimal errors



• Strong communication and follow-up skills



WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT





• This is not an assistant position



• This is not a beginner role



• This is not a “learn as you go” position







You are expected to take ownership of this function and execute with accuracy.



SUCCESS IN THIS ROLE LOOKS LIKE





• Top-selling products remain in stock



• Restocks are planned proactively (not reactively)



• No pre-orders are opened without confirmed production



• Production timelines are tracked and communicated clearly



• Shopify backend is accurate and well-managed



• Weekly reports directly support sales growth





APPLICATION QUESTIONS (REQUIRED) Please submit answers together with your updated resume at 





Please answer the following:



How do you determine when a product needs to be restocked?

What data or metrics do you use to forecast inventory demand?

Have you used Inventory Planner or a similar tool? Explain how.

How do you prevent stockouts on top-selling products?

Describe your experience working with overseas manufacturers.

What steps do you take before opening a product for pre-order?



FINAL NOTE





We are looking for someone reliable, proactive, and experienced. If you are someone who needs constant direction or training, this role is not the right fit.

Due to time constraints, I’m no longer able to manage the full production process myself, but I remain deeply passionate about the channel and its long-term vision. I’m looking for a Video Editor with motion design capability to produce high-quality, engaging history videos in a documentary style.

Responsibilities

Historical images and footage

Motion graphics and transitions

title: Video Editor for Performance Ads (Full-Time & Long term only)
description:
We are looking for a high-performance Video Editor specialized in Meta Ads.



Your main mission is simple but critical:



---Take winning ads and create multiple strong variations by:



*Changing hooks

*Changing pacing

*Swapping b-rolls



Creating clearly different versions for Meta testing



--This is NOT YouTube editing.

--This is direct response advertising.



Output expectations:

*8–12 variations per day (depending on complexity)

*Typical video length: 60s

*Deadline: often 24h turnaround





Follow very clear SOPs (Notion + examples)



Focus on:

*Scroll-stopping hooks

*Retention

*Fast-paced editing



Our ads are in French. You do NOT need to speak French.



You will receive:

*English instructions

*Translations

*References and examples

*Clear SOPs (Notion)



**Your job is execution, not copywriting.**





--REQUIREMENTS (READ CAREFULLY)



You are a good fit ONLY IF:

*You have experience editing Meta /
 Ads



You understand:

*Hooks

*Pattern interrupts

*Retention editing

*You can create 10+ variations per day

*You are fast, organized, and disciplined

*You can follow instructions exactly

*You accept feedback and iterations



NOT A FIT IF:

*You only edit YouTube or cinematic videos

*You prefer “beautiful” edits over performance

*You need creative freedom to invent concepts

*You are slow or inconsistent







****COMPENSATION****

-Salary: $1,000 – $1,500 per month (USD, depending on skills & speed)

-Long-term collaboration

-Stable workload

-Performance-based bonuses possible





--How to Apply

Fill out this application form: 



--Only candidates who apply via this form will be considered.

Hadley Designs is seeking a Creative Design Assistant & Digital Illustrator to join our creative team and play a pivotal role in crafting high-quality, visually stunning designs that enhance our brand presence. If you are a creative powerhouse proficient in Adobe Illustrator with a keen eye for design, we want you to help bring our vision to life.



This is a hands-on, dynamic role ideal for a creative professional passionate about design and eager to make an impact on a fast-growing company. You’ll collaborate with leadership, work on high-priority assignments, and ensure the seamless execution of design projects that elevate Hadley Designs' brand.



About Hadley Designs

Hadley Designs is a family-founded, female-led brand committed to shaping brighter futures through creativity and purposeful design. We create screen-free learning tools that spark curiosity, build confidence, and transform everyday 
nts into meaningful growth. Each product is hand-drawn, crafted with input from teachers, parents, and child development experts, ensuring learning is joyful, engaging, and accessible.



Our mission is simple: to help families and educators unlock children’s potential with tools that inspire curiosity, strengthen connections, and support learning without screens. Trusted by over two million customers worldwide, we are passionate about designing experiences that make children feel supported, capable, and excited to learn.



If you’re looking for a company that designs with purpose and builds with heart, you’ll feel right at home here.



How You’ll Win at Hadley Designs



We believe in structured creativity. Success in this role means owning your projects and delivering high-quality designs that resonate with our customers while maintaining our visual standards. We’re looking for someone who thrives in a creative environment, is proactive, and possesses a strong attention to detail.



Every Design Assistant is expected to:



Learn the Playbook – Understand our design standards, expectations, and creative processes.

Apply the Playbook – Execute designs consistently, meeting project requirements and adhering to brand guidelines.

Enhance the Playbook – Suggest creative improvements and innovative design strategies after mastering the basic framework.

We value innovation grounded in proven execution and a clear understanding of design principles.



What You’ll Own



-Creation of high-quality, error-free design assets for advertising and marketing campaigns.

-Maintaining consistency in typography, color schemes, and layout across all platforms.

-Regular updates and design of storefront assets for Amazon, Shopify, and other platforms.

-Designing A+ Content templates and custom brand image ads.

-Handling urgent design needs for product launches and special projects.

-Working closely with leadership to ensure deadlines are met and projects are delivered on time.



What Success Looks Like

Within your first 90 days, you will:



-Learn and execute Hadley Designs’ design standards with precision.

-Contribute fresh design ideas that align with the brand and improve our current processes.

-Deliver high-quality artwork that meets or exceeds expectations and is on-brand.

-Support Becca and leadership with urgent creative tasks and maintain consistency in all design work.



Apply Now, Lead Creativity, and Shape Our Future.



Requirements

Who You Are:



-2+ years of professional experience in graphic design or related fields, with a strong portfolio.

-Proficient in Adobe Illustrator and Photoshop, with a strong grasp of CMYK color management and print-ready file preparation.

-Excellent attention to detail and ability to produce high-quality visuals with minimal revisions.

-Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.

-Comfortable receiving and implementing feedback while maintaining a high standard of work.

-Creative, collaborative, and self-driven, motivated by quality and continuous improvement.



Benefits

Why You’ll Thrive Here:



-Join a creative, high-growth company with an established 10+ year track record.

-Own significant projects and make an impact from day one.

-Flexibility to work remotely with a flexible schedule.

-Opportunities for personal development and direct interaction with the Chief Creative Officer.

-A culture of collaboration, trust, and innovation, where your contributions are valued.



Apply Now

If you're a passionate graphic designer who thrives in a dynamic and creative environment, we'd love to hear from you. Apply today to join Hadley Designs and help shape the future of our brand!



P.S. If you don’t hear from us within four weeks of your application, please consider that as our decision to move forward with other candidates, but we truly appreciate your interest in Hadley Designs.



To formally apply : apply here

title: Hotel and Short Term Rental Revenue Manager (Virtual Assistant)
description:
Role Overview:

Big Easy Management is seeking a highly analytical Revenue Manager (Virtual Assistant) to support pricing, market analysis, and performance optimization across our portfolio of short-term rentals and boutique hotel properties.

This role is responsible for digging into data, identifying opportunities, and helping drive revenue performance at the unit level. The right candidate is proactive, detail-oriented, and constantly looking for ways to improve pricing, positioning, and listing performance.

This is not a reporting-only role. This is also an analysis and opportunity identification role.

*It is important to review the Application Instructions at the end of this document.



Key Responsibilities:



Performance Monitoring:

- Track and monitor past, current, and forward-looking performance of individual properties

- Analyze occupancy, ADR, revenue trends, and booking pace

- Identify underperforming units and recommend specific actions



Comp Set Creation + Competitive Analysis:

- Build and maintain comp sets for each property

- Analyze competitor pricing, listings, amenities, and positioning

- Identify competitive advantages that can be replicated or improved upon



Listing + Revenue Optimization:

- Identify ways to improve listing performance (titles, descriptions, photos, amenities) based on analysis of properties that outperform in their compset

- Recommend pricing adjustments based on demand, seasonality, comp sets, and market trends

- Ensure listings are competitively priced within their market

- Identify opportunities to improve unit performance through amenity upgrades or adjustments based on competitor analysis & guest expectations/feedback

- Track impact of improvements on performance



Promotions + Demand Generation:

- Assist in creating and managing promotional strategies and discount codes for OTAs & direct bookings

- Identify opportunities to drive occupancy during low-demand periods



Market + Neighborhood Analysis:

- Monitor what’s happening in the neighborhoods where our properties operate

- Track events, demand drivers, and seasonality shifts

- Provide insights that inform pricing and positioning decisions



Reporting + Opportunity Tracking:

- Create and maintain reporting that tracks:

- Performance trends

- Changes made and their impact



Market Comparisons:

- Clearly highlight opportunities, risks, and recommended actions

- Focus on actionable insights, not just data





Qualifications:

- This is not an entry-level generic VA role

- Experience in revenue management for hotels and/or short-term rentals (required)

- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

- Ability to translate data into clear recommendations

- Experience with pricing tools (Wheelhouse, PriceLabs, or similar preferred)

- Familiarity with Airbnb and property management systems (Hostaway preferred)

- Strong attention to detail and organization

- Applicants must complete the required steps to be considered

- Incomplete applications are not reviewed



Bonus:

Knowledge of and/or experience in the New Orleans market



Reporting Structure:

- Reports directly to the Director of Product

- Works closely with internal teams to support performance improvements



What Success Looks Like:

- Consistent identification of revenue and performance opportunities

- Improved performance across individual units and comp sets

- Clear, actionable reporting that drives decisions

- Proactive recommendations, not reactive reporting



Application Instructions:

 To be considered for this role, all applicants must complete the Criteria Corp assessment and upload a single PDF during the application process. Link to Criteria Corp Here- 

1. Your PDF must include:

2. Your resume

3. Written answers to the questions below

4. Your response to the short exercise



Please begin your document with the phrase: Revenue is in the details.

Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be considered.



Application Questions:

1. What revenue management experience do you have in hotels and/or short-term rentals?

2. What tools have you used? Please include any experience with Airbnb, Hostaway, Wheelhouse, PriceLabs, AirDNA, Google Sheets, Slack, Gmail, or similar platforms.

3. Tell us about one pricing, listing, amenity, or promotional opportunity you identified that improved performance. What did you find, and what action was taken?

4. What hourly pay are you seeking, what is your weekly availability, and are you willing to work weekends if needed?



Short Exercise:

Please answer the following prompt in 5 to 10 bullet points within the same PDF document:

You are reviewing a 3-bedroom short-term rental with lower-than-expected occupancy over the next 30 days. Nearby comparable listings appear to have stronger reviews, better amenities, and better listing presentation. What would you review first, and what actions would you recommend?

title: Technical Meta Ads & GHL Specialist (Financial Services Clients)
description:
The Role:



We are a fast-paced marketing agency helping wealth management companies scale their businesses. We are looking for a highly technical, experienced Meta Ads and Workflow Automation Specialist to take ownership of our client onboarding, technical implementation, and campaign management.



How we divide the work: You will work directly with myself (Sam Burrows, Founder of Burrows & Partners). My job is the creative vision and growing the agency. Your job is looking after client performance, fixing problems, and handling the flawless technical implementation of our systems.



While we have a creative team and proven templates, creative ability is always a bonus in today's Meta landscape. However, your core superpower must be technical problem-solving. If you love diving into data, troubleshooting broken funnels, setting up seamless automations, and dealing with the tech headaches that I don't enjoy, this is the perfect role for you.



We are looking for someone who thrives on efficiency, speed, and accuracy, and who wants to work for a loyal, supportive, but demanding boss who heavily rewards top performers.



What You Will Do:



•

Client Onboarding & Funnel Architecture: A huge part of this role is onboarding new clients. You will build landing pages in GoHighLevel (GHL) following our SOPs, set up
 sequences, and map out CRM workflows.



•

Automation Implementation: Connect the dots flawlessly. You will set up complex automations using Zapier, webhooks, and native integrations to ensure leads flow perfectly from ad to CRM.



•

Meta Ads Management & Troubleshooting: Launch, manage, and optimise Meta ad campaigns. When tracking breaks or a CPA spikes, you are the first line of defence to diagnose and fix the issue.



•

Navigating Meta Policy & Restrictions: You will be dealing with Meta Business Manager daily. We operate in a regulated industry (Financial Services). You must have deep experience dealing with Meta's advertising policies, navigating account restrictions, and communicating effectively with Meta Marketing Pros. Ideally, you know the Meta policy landscape better than I do.



Who You Are:



•

A Meta Veteran (3+ Years Required): You have a minimum of 3 years of hands-on Meta Ads experience. You have dealt with Meta Marketing Pros, handled account restrictions, and understand the importance of Business Manager health. Experience operating within Meta's financial services advertising policies and Special Ad Categories is preferred.



•

A Technical Troubleshooter: You genuinely enjoy fixing broken systems. You can build multi-step workflows in GoHighLevel and Zapier in your sleep.



•

Creative-Aware: You understand that creative is the biggest driver of Meta ad performance right now. While you are a technical implementer first, any creative copywriting or ad design ability is a bonus.



•

Fast & Efficient: You take a brief, ask the right questions, and implement it quickly and accurately. Occasional weekend monitoring may be required, but you will be compensated and looked after very well for your dedication.



•

Compliant: You understand that working in our environment means adhering to strict operational security (e.g., using dedicated devices for BM access, no VPNs while accessing Meta).



Requirements:



•

Minimum 3 years of proven experience managing and scaling Meta Ads.



•

Deep technical expertise in GoHighLevel (GHL), CRM workflows, and automation (Zapier/Webhooks).



•

Proven experience dealing with Meta account restrictions, policy flags, and Meta support.



•

Bonus: Creative ability (copywriting, understanding what makes a winning ad creative).



•

Bonus: Meta Certified Media Buying Professional status.



•

Bonus: Previous experience running ads in the Financial Services vertical.



How to Apply:



We only hire people with exceptional attention to detail. Do not send a generic cover letter; it will be deleted.



To apply, please fill out our application form: 





And then submit on here with your CV.



We look forward to meeting you!

title: Remote Land Sourcing & Research Specialist

title: Sales Associate Manager (Analyst) – Ticketing
description:
TO BE CONSIDERED, PLEASE ATTACH YOUR CV AND DESCRIBE YOUR EXPERIENCE IN TICKETING



Company Description



Our company specializes in developing innovative solutions within the live entertainment industry, focusing on enhancing fan experiences and engagement. By creating B2B and B2C systems, the company aims to provide tools and services that empower promoters, marketplaces, artists, and marketers to better serve their audiences. The company also explores cross-border inventory and content distribution opportunities while pioneering e-commerce concepts for underserved fans. Driven by innovation, the company is passionate about advancing the live entertainment ecosystem.

This roles sits within the Ticket Distribution/ Broker business.



Role Description



We are hiring a Sales Associate Manager to support the Sales Manager at a fast-growing ticketing business focused on distributing concerts, sports and other major live events across the UK, Europe and Asia.



This is a junior commercial role within the Sales team. The person will support on inventory acquisition, category management, and event analysis, while developing into a future Sales Manager with stronger ownership across pricing, category expertise, and new event identification.



We are looking for someone who is smart, highly analytical, detail-oriented, and eager to learn quickly. You must already have at least 1 year of experience in ticketing





Main responsibilities



Acquire inventory and/or coordinate inventory acquisition across key events

Support ticket category management

Help build ownership over one category, vertical or smaller market

Research events, supply/demand patterns, pricing and market behaviour

Support commercial analysis and business cases

Work closely with the Sales Manager on execution and opportunity sizing

Maintain trackers and keep commercial activity organised





Requirements



At least 1 year of experience in secondary market ticketing

Good understanding of inventory acquisition, management and pricing

Strong analytical mindset

Very comfortable with Excel/ Data visualisation tools

Strong attention to detail and reliability

Fast learner with good commercial judgment

Experience with Viagogo, StubHub, Skybox or similar tools is a strong plus

Background in Mathematics or another quantitative field is a big plus





This is a great role for someone who wants to build a long-term commercial career in ticketing and live entertainment, and grow into a broader Sales Manager role over time.



What we offer



Competitive package with a significant variable component tied to commercial results (2x OTE).

Opportunity to work and get mentored by the Directors and be a core part of the company’s hyper-growth story having the support form the rest of the team.

Remote-first setup with flexibility, while following Europe (GMT) working hours.

Best in industry benefits: HMO, remote work allowance, PTO, paid coworking, learning & development budget, 1x event experience per year.

A builder culture: space to experiment, propose ideas and shape how we make commercial decisions.

Exposure to multiple markets (UK, Europe, Asia, America) and categories (music, sports, premium events).

title: Full-Stack Software Developer (Automation, Ticketing)
