title: Content Operations VA
description:
The role

Around Finance is a modern Irish accountancy firm. Our founder Richie Lennon creates all our content — blogs, LinkedIn posts, videos, and a monthly newsletter. Your job is to take that content and get it live. Formatted, scheduled, and published properly across WordPress, social media, and HubSpot.



Right now this is primarily an execution role. Richie writes and films, you make it happen. But if you have good content instincts and want to contribute ideas over time that door is open. We want someone great at the job first who can grow from there.



Once content is approved it is your responsibility to ensure it goes live correctly and on time. You own that layer completely.



What you will do every week

• Review Richie's voice note transcripts in Notion daily — categorise by content pillar and sector, flag the strongest ideas for his Friday session

• Format approved blogs in WordPress — headings, Yoast SEO fields, featured image from ChatGPT, correct categories, tags, and internal links

• Schedule LinkedIn posts across personal profile and company page in Metricool — personal content reposts to company page 24 hours later

• Upload reels and schedule Instagram and
 posts on the agreed weekly calendar

• Keep WordPress ticking — plugin updates, broken link checks, minor page edits when briefed

• Send a short weekly summary every Thursday — what is live, what is scheduled, anything flagged

• Prepare a Friday review pack — all content ready for Richie's approval before anything goes live



Once a month:

• Build the monthly
 newsletter in HubSpot — pull from the month's blogs and content, format for send, proof it, and submit for Richie's approval before it goes out



Tasks are assigned in batches and expected to be completed within 24 to 48 hours. The content calendar needs to stay 7 days ahead at all times.



Tools

The main tools are Notion, WordPress, Yoast SEO, Metricool, HubSpot, ChatGPT, Canva, and Google Drive. If you haven't used some of these specifically that's fine — experience in Buffer, Hootsuite, Mailchimp, Airtable, or similar tools means you'll pick up the specifics quickly. HubSpot experience is a bonus — willingness to learn it fast is a must.



What we're looking for

• At least 2 years as a content VA, social media VA, or digital marketing assistant

• Previous experience with US, Australian, or UK clients — strong preference, not optional

• Background in professional services, marketing agencies, or tech companies is a real advantage — you already understand the pace and the standards

• Excellent written English — you'll be formatting and publishing content under a named professional's brand

• Available and reactive — you respond within working hours, flag problems early, and don't go quiet for days

• Process-driven — you follow a brief cleanly without needing constant direction



We'd like to see examples of your work — a portfolio, a sample, a short Loom walkthrough, or anything that shows us how you work. We're not expecting access to a previous client's private site. Just show us what you can do.



This role lives or dies on consistency under low supervision. If you need to be chased, go quiet for days, or find process-driven work frustrating — this won't work.



What success looks like

• Content is always live on time

• The calendar is always 7 days ahead

• No errors in published content or the newsletter

• Richie never has to follow up on execution

• Weekly summaries are short, clear, and accurate

• When something goes wrong you flag it before he notices — not after



Not a fit

• You're looking for a full-time role — this is around 5 hours a week

• You've only worked for local clients or entry-level accounts

• You go quiet for days or need a lot of direction to get started

• You find process-driven execution work frustrating



Paid trial task

Before any offer we ask shortlisted candidates to complete a paid two-hour trial. You'll format one blog post in a WordPress test environment with Yoast fields, a ChatGPT image, and internal links. You'll also categorise three voice note transcripts in a Notion template we provide.



Paid at the agreed hourly rate. We're looking for accuracy and the ability to follow a brief without a lot of back and forth.



How to apply

To apply, submit your application through this platform. Start your message with the word FOUNDERS — applications that don't open with this word won't be read

 Then tell us who you've worked for (especially US, AUS, or UK clients), share examples of your work, list your tools and experience level, and tell us your availability and rate.

title: HIRING Full-Time Video Editor for 7 Figures Supplement Brand
description:
HIRING Full-Time Video Editor for 7 Figures Supplement Brand



About Us

We’re a fast-growing DTC brand in the health supplement space, scaling to 30K days with performance-driven creatives. Every ad we launch is backed by data and crafted to convert. We’re looking for a video editor who not only edits but also thinks like a marketer - someone who wants to grow, push limits, and create winning ads at scale.



The Role

We’re hiring a Video Editor who can consistently deliver high-performing Meta video ads (30–120s). This is a long-term, remote role with $4 start salary which increase if hit rate is on target. Also introducing performance bonuses in 2nd month.



What You’ll Do

- Edit high-converting ads for Meta & Google (30–120s)

- Apply strong hooks, pacing, captions, and transitions

- Work with UGC, b-roll, and AI-generated content (Heygen, ElevenLabs, Nano Banana and VEO3)

- Test UGC-style videos, TikTok-style ads, AI visuals, and VSLs

- Analyze performance data & iterate quickly based on KPIs (hook rate, hold rate, CVR)

- Collaborate closely with the Creative Manager + other editors to push volume

- Source raw footage & voiceovers to bring concepts to life

- Experiment with new formats, trends, and storytelling angles



You Need

- Experience editing performance-driven Meta ads (UGC/direct response)

- Proven portfolio of short-form, direct response ads

- Strong creative eye and ability to deliver 15 ad concepts per week

- Understanding of marketing psychology, hooks, and consumer behavior

- Hunger to grow — willing to test, fail, and iterate fast



What You’ll Get

- Base salary: $4 per hour (start salary that increases if performance is on target) + performance bonuses

- Fully remote role

- Direct collaboration with a high-performing editors, strategists and Founder

- Managed under a dedicated Creative Manager who oversees editors and strategy



How to Apply

Send us a link to your portfolio or past performance ads

title: Property Account Manager
description:
Job Title: Utilities Setup and Accounts Clean-Up Coordinator

Job Type: Fixed-term contract

Contract Length: 4 weeks fixed

Working Hours: Monday to Friday, aligned to UK business hours



Contract Fee: PH Peso 40,000 fixed for the 4-week contract

Bonus: PH Peso 10,000 completion bonus based on successful delivery of agreed milestones, weekly handovers, and final project completion

Start Date: Immediate





Job Description

We are a UK property business managing a shared housing portfolio across multiple houses. We are looking for one highly organised and reliable coordinator based in the Philippines to take ownership of a short-term but important utilities and account clean-up project.



The purpose of this role is to review, organise, set up, and clean up utility-related accounts across up to 21 houses. This includes electricity, gas, water, internet, and council tax-related coordination where required.



This is not a long-term admin role. This is a focused 4-week contract for someone who can come in, sort out the utility structure properly, clean up old issues, contact suppliers, help put payment systems in place, research better rates, and leave behind a clean and usable operating system for the business.



You will be expected to work through active and old account issues, chase suppliers properly, set up direct debits, help organise payment plans for debts where needed, and create clear trackers and SOPs for future management.

By the end of the contract, the utility infrastructure should be properly organised, documented, and ready for ongoing use.





What You Will Be Responsible For

You will be responsible for:



Setting up new utility accounts for up to 20 houses, including electricity, gas, water, internet, and council tax-related coordination where required

Reviewing and cleaning up old utility accounts to make sure records are accurate and issues are identified properly and setting up payment plan

Contacting suppliers directly to resolve account issues, confirm balances, update details, and move accounts into the correct structure via calls

Helping arrange payment plans for debts or arrears where needed and ensuring these are recorded clearly

Setting up direct debits and payment arrangements correctly for active accounts

Researching better rates, tariffs, and suppliers where relevant

Negotiating workable arrangements or improved deals with suppliers where possible

Building a clean utility account structure that is organised, trackable, and usable going forward

Preparing clear trackers, notes, and records so the business has full visibility across all utility accounts

Creating a simple SOP on spreadsheet and word doc and final handover so the utility setup can be maintained properly after the contract ends



Project Deliverables

By the end of the 4-week contract, we expect:

A full list of all houses and their utility account status

New accounts opened where required

Old accounts reviewed and cleaned up where possible

Debt positions identified and payment plans arranged where needed

Direct debits set up and recorded

Supplier contact notes and account updates maintained clearly

Better tariff or supplier opportunities flagged where relevant

A clean master utility tracker for future use

A simple SOP showing how utility accounts should be managed going forward

A final handover showing what has been completed, what is pending, and what future management should look like





How the Project Will Be Managed:

This contract will be managed in weekly phases with clear handovers.

Each week, you will be expected to provide:

A summary of what has been completed

A list of what remains open

Any blockers or supplier delays

Notes on what action has been taken

Priorities for the following week

The project is expected to be completed properly within the 4-week contract period.





Who This Role Is For:

This role is for someone who is highly organised, good at follow-up, comfortable dealing with suppliers, and able to bring structure to messy account situations.

You should be confident handling multiple houses, multiple accounts, and multiple supplier conversations at the same time.

You should be able to work independently, keep records properly, and push issues forward without constant supervision.

This role suits someone who is practical, detail-focused, calm under pressure, and good at getting admin and supplier issues resolved properly.





Essential Requirements:

You must:

Have experience in account setup, supplier coordination, admin clean-up, utilities handling, or similar operational support work

Be comfortable managing multiple properties, accounts, or suppliers at the same time

Have strong written and spoken English

Be highly organised and detail-focused

Be confident following up with suppliers and pushing issues toward resolution

Be comfortable using spreadsheets, trackers, and written process notes

Be available for a fixed 4-week project aligned to UK business hours

Have a stable remote setup with reliable internet and laptop





Preferred Experience:



It is preferred if you have experience in one or more of the following:

UK property or portfolio administration

Utility account coordination

Council tax or supplier handling

Debt follow-up or payment plan setup

Process building or SOP creation

Property operations, facilities admin, or infrastructure coordination



Compensation Structure:

This role is offered on a 4-week fixed contract fee of  PH Peso 40,000.

In addition, there is  PH Peso 10,000 completion bonus available based on successful delivery of agreed milestones, weekly handovers, and final project completion.

The completion bonus is not automatic and will only be paid if the work is completed properly, handed over clearly, and the agreed project scope is delivered to a satisfactory standard.



How to Apply

 your CV to 
 with the subject line:

Utilities Setup and Accounts

Along with your CV, send a short audio / video introduction, answer the following:



Have you handled multiple properties, suppliers, or account issues at the same time

Give one real example of a messy account, supplier, or admin issue you helped resolve

Why do you believe you are suitable for a 4-week project-based role with clear delivery targets

title: Senior Video Editor [ Pacific Standard Time ]
description:
About Veritas Vans

Veritas Vans builds the most trusted accessories for Starlink Mini and Starlink Standard. Our mission is to give people the freedom to work, explore, and stay connected anywhere. From Baja coastlines to deep forest trails, our customers rely on our gear for capability, protection, and unlimited connection.



Your role is to help visually tell this story across all platforms.



Role Overview

We are hiring a Senior Video Editor who can own our long-form YouTube content, high-retention short-form content, and graphic design needs. You must be highly experienced, creative, fast, and able to work autonomously within a growing brand.



Responsibilities



Video Editing – Long Form

- Edit 6–15 minute YouTube videos with strong retention, pacing, and narrative clarity.

- Add b-roll, overlays, captions, music, and brand-consistent graphics.

- Build engaging openings with proven YouTube hook methods.



Video Editing – Short Form

- Execute the Hook ? Retain ? Reward method for Reels, TikTok, and Shorts.

- Add dynamic subtitles, zooms, cuts, 
 inserts, and pacing adjustments.

- Produce scroll-stopping, high-retention content.



Graphic Design & Photo Editing

- Create YouTube thumbnails and graphic assets.

- Design text treatments, overlays, and promotional visuals.

- Edit lifestyle and product photos for multi-platform use.



Brand Storytelling

- Align all edits with our brand themes: freedom, connection, capability, control.

- Contribute creative ideas and concepts for future video content.



AI-Driven Editing & Workflow Optimization

- Use AI tools (ChatGPT, Adobe AI, Topaz, Descript, etc.) as a core part of your editing workflow.

- Generate high-performing hooks, captions, and on-screen text callouts using AI.

- Use AI to research and improve storytelling, pacing, and content structure.

- Continuously explore and implement new AI workflows to improve output quality, speed, and efficiency.

- Leverage AI to enhance both creative output and production throughput, not just assist basic tasks.



Cross-Platform Optimization

- Edit in multiple aspect ratios: 16:9, 9:16, 4:5, 1:1.

- Prepare final deliverables in organized, structured folders.

- Optimize content for YouTube, Instagram, TikTok,
.



Collaboration

- Work closely with the content team and leadership.

- Follow SOPs and meet deadlines consistently.

- Join scheduled calls proactively (no reminders required)

- Be punctual and prepared for feedback sessions



Work Discipline & Delivery Standards

- Be actively available and responsive during working hours (PST schedule).

- Address tasks, revisions, and team communication within working hours.

- Meet deadlines consistently and communicate early if delays occur.

- Submit deliverables early in the workday whenever possible to allow time for review and revisions.



Quality & Output Standards

- Deliver edits that are near publish-ready on first submission.

- Ensure drafts require minimal revisions and are not incomplete or partially executed.

- Perform a full self-review before submission to ensure:

- all feedback is applied

- no new issues are introduced

- output meets brand and quality standards

- Maintain consistently high production quality across all deliverables.



Note:

Include the phrase “Senior Video Editor Pro” in your subject line. Applications missing this phrase will not be considered. Use at least 5AP for your application.



Required Skills



- Ability to work graveyard shift (PST hours)

- 3–5+ years professional video editing experience

- Expert in Adobe Premiere Pro

- Strong skills in After Effects, Photoshop, Illustrator, or Figma

- Proven experience editing YouTube content and high-performing short-form videos

- Strong understanding of retention, pacing, story structure, and attention engineering

- Experience creating high-converting thumbnails

- Strong understanding of modern content trends across YouTube, Instagram, and TikTok

- Ability to edit lifestyle, outdoor, product, and UGC-style content



AI & Workflow Skills (Required)

- Strong proficiency in using AI tools (ChatGPT, Adobe AI, etc.) within editing workflows

- Ability to generate hooks, captions, and on-screen text using AI

- Experience using AI to improve storytelling, pacing, and video performance

- Ability to research and implement new AI workflows to improve efficiency and output quality



Work Ethic & Discipline

- Highly reliable, responsive, and accountable

- Strong attention to detail and ability to self-review work

- Ability to follow feedback precisely and minimize repeated revisions



Bonus Experience

- Motion graphics or animation skills.

- Experience in e-commerce, outdoor brands, or creator channels.

- Understanding of YouTube SEO (titles, thumbnails, keywords).

- Ability to generate ideas for hooks, storylines, and visual treatments.



What We Offer

- Long-term employment with growth potential.

- Competitive salary based on experience.

- Consistent weekly workload.

- Direct impact on a fast-scaling global brand.

- Creative freedom and an opportunity to lead our video direction.



How to Apply



Submit the following:

- Resume

- Short explanation of why you’re the right fit

- Brief explanation of how you use AI in your editing workflow

- 1 example where you improved video performance (retention, CTR, etc.)

- Portfolio of your best long-form and short-form edits

- Links to YouTube channels or brands you’ve edited for



Learn About Us:









Website:

title: Personal Travel & Executive Assistant
description:
I’m a Singapore-based retired executive who travels frequently and manages several rental properties and investments. I’m tech-savvy and a heavy user of AI tools, especially Perplexity. I’m looking for a long-term, part-time assistant (around 10 hours/week) who will start with a strong focus on planning and booking international personal travel, then gradually take on more property-management and executive support as you learn the systems and context.



You will plan and book multi-city international trips (flights, hotels/Airbnb, trains, cars, activities), prepare clear travel packs, help with visas and travel disruptions, and keep everything well organised. Over time you’ll maintain simple property and expense spreadsheets, support tenant/admin tasks, and use AI tools to research options, compare trade-offs, and present clear recommendations. The role requires strong analytical skills, excellent written English, high attention to detail, and the ability to solve problems independently—not just follow checklists. Occasional business travel to Singapore may be required by mutual agreement.



Requirements

3+ years in travel consultancy/agency, EA/PA, or VA/operations roles with regular international travel planning.

Strong experience with complex itineraries and major airline/hotel/Airbnb/rail/car platforms.

Confident with spreadsheets and cloud tools.

Willing and able to use AI tools (Perplexity, etc.) daily.

Proven discretion with sensitive information.

Bachelor’s degree (business, tourism/hospitality, or related) is preferred, but strong equivalent experience is also welcome.

Approximately 10 hours/week during SG/PH daytime; remote from the Philippines or similar timezone.

A dedicated Apple MacBook will be provided.

title: Klarna & PayPal Dispute Expert
description:
We are an e-commerce company focused on providing excellent products to our customers, primarily through platforms like Klarna and PayPal. As our business continues to grow, we are seeking an experienced Klarna & PayPal Dispute Agent to join our team. You will play a key role in managing and resolving payment disputes efficiently to ensure a smooth customer experience.



Responsibilities:



•Review and manage Klarna and PayPal disputes in a timely and professional manner.

Investigate chargebacks and customer claims by analyzing transaction details, policies, and customer history.

Communicate with customers, Klarna, and PayPal to resolve disputes, ensuring compliance with company policies.

Prepare and submit documentation to Klarna and PayPal to defend chargeback cases.

Track the status of ongoing disputes and keep relevant records up to date.

Ensure that customer queries related to payment disputes are resolved to maintain customer satisfaction.

Provide clear and concise reports on dispute resolutions and outcomes.

Suggest improvements in internal dispute handling procedures based on experience.

Skills & Qualifications:



Proven experience in handling Klarna and PayPal disputes, chargebacks, and refunds.

Strong knowledge of e-commerce transaction systems and policies for Klarna, PayPal, and other payment processors.

Excellent communication skills with the ability to handle sensitive situations empathetically.

Ability to analyze transaction data and identify patterns in disputes.

Detail-oriented with strong organizational skills to manage multiple disputes concurrently.

Proficient in using dispute resolution tools and platforms (Shopify, Freshdesk, PayPal, Klarna dispute portals, etc.).

Problem-solving mindset, capable of identifying issues and providing effective resolutions.

Strong knowledge of e-commerce and consumer protection laws.

A background in customer service is highly beneficial.

Preferred Experience:



Experience working in a fast-paced e-commerce environment.

Familiarity with dispute resolution strategies to minimize chargeback rates and protect business revenue.

Multilingual capabilities are a plus (particularly Dutch, English, and German).





What We Offer:



Flexible working hours with the possibility of remote work.

A dynamic and growing company with opportunities for advancement.

A supportive and friendly work environment.





If you are a proactive, detail-oriented individual with expertise in managing Klarna and PayPal disputes, we would love to hear from you! Please send your resume and a cover letter detailing your experience with payment disputes.

title: 3D Shape Designer (Blender / 3D Modeling)
description:
3D Shape Designer (Blender / 3D Modeling)

 Location: Remote – Philippines



Contract Position

We’re looking for someone who is naturally good at 3D shapes.

If you enjoy building smooth curves, sculpting surfaces, and making forms that feel balanced and clean — this role is for you.

You might use Blender. You might use another 3D tool. What matters most is your eye. You understand convex and concave shapes. You can create flowing transitions. You know when a curve looks wrong — and you fix it.

We design real physical products. Your role will focus on developing and refining the 3D form — the visual shape and surface quality.



What You’ll Do



Create and refine 3D forms and surfaces

Build smooth transitions between curves

Adjust proportions and shape flow

Work closely with the project lead to refine designs

Translate aesthetic forms into structured 3D models





What We’re Looking For



Experience with Blender or other 3D modeling software

Strong sense of form and proportion

Ability to create clean, smooth surfaces

Attention to detail in curves and transitions

Clear English communication

Reliable internet connection





This Is a Good Fit If You:



Enjoy sculpting or shaping objects in 3D

Care about how things look and feel visually

Notice when lines and curves are slightly off

Have personal 3D projects you’re proud of





To Apply

Please send:

A short introduction

Images or a portfolio of your 3D work

A brief explanation of one project you enjoyed creating

We’re looking for someone who truly understands shape.
